column2: Virtual assistant for services - first step towards artificial intelligence in diplomacy
column4: Citizens and businesses interacting with diplomatic and consular services often face complex procedures and time-zone constraints, leading to delays and information gaps. The Italian Ministry of Foreign Affairs sought to improve service accessibility and consistency across its global network. The objective of this pilot is to provide continuous, multilingual guidance for consular and administrative services. The implementation introduces a virtual assistant available on ministry, embassy and consulate websites, supporting six languages and operating around the clock. The assistant guides users through structured decision trees based on frequently asked questions and common procedures, optimised during development through analysis of historical enquiries. Initially deployed across 20 pilot locations, the system integrates with existing digital service channels. The pilot improved response availability, reduced pressure on frontline staff and enhanced user satisfaction. Qualitative benefits include more consistent service delivery, improved accessibility for international users and a foundation for future digital transformation in diplomatic services.
column8: Italian Ministry of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation
column11: Executive and legislative organs, financial and fiscal affairs, external affairs.

column2: OLI Chatbot across spanish airports
column4: Airports manage complex passenger flows and high volumes of enquiries related to flights, terminals, and services, particularly during peak travel periods. AENA, the operator of Spanish airports, introduced OLI to improve passenger experience and reduce pressure on staff. This use case involves airport authorities, passengers, airlines, and service providers, with the objective of delivering timely, accurate information across multiple channels. OLI is implemented as a multilingual digital assistant integrated with operational airport systems, including flight information displays and service directories. It is accessible via WhatsApp, the AENA website and app, and social media platforms, enabling passengers to receive real-time updates on flights, boarding gates, terminal navigation, and available services. The assistant continuously retrieves and delivers live operational data in multiple languages. By April 2024, OLI had interacted with approximately 940,000 users, supporting its rollout to additional airports. The impact includes improved passenger satisfaction, reduced queues at information desks, and more efficient airport operations. OLI enhances accessibility, supports scalable customer service, and contributes to smoother passenger journeys across Spain’s airport network.

column2: Spain’s Social Security administration: Using AI to deliver benefits to citizens more efficiently with the ISSA Social Security Virtual Assistant
column4: Spain’s Social Security administration handles millions of citizen enquiries each year, creating heavy workloads and long waiting times for routine information requests. To improve service delivery, the administration introduced a virtual assistant to provide immediate access to official guidance. The use case involves citizens, Social Security staff, and public service managers, with the objective of improving accessibility, reducing administrative burden, and ensuring consistent information delivery. The solution is implemented as an online conversational assistant that provides 24/7 responses on frequently requested procedures and benefits. Initially covering 37 core services, it retrieves information from official Social Security databases and guidance repositories. Integrated into digital service channels, it allows citizens to self-serve for common enquiries while redirecting complex cases to human agents. In its first month alone, the assistant handled over 2 million interactions, demonstrating high demand and scalability. The impact includes faster access to information, reduced call-centre pressure, and improved operational efficiency. Citizens benefit from immediate, reliable responses, while the administration reallocates staff time to higher-value tasks, delivering measurable improvements in public service performance.

column2: Kit Consulting – AI-Enabled Grant Processing
column4: Public funding programmes supporting SMEs often involve extensive document verification, which can slow payments and increase administrative costs. Spain’s Kit Consulting programme sought to modernise this process while maintaining rigorous oversight. The use case involves Red.es, digital consultants, SMEs, and auditors, with the objective of accelerating document review, improving quality control, and ensuring transparency. The solution is implemented through a digital platform that automatically analyses submitted documentation, detecting plagiarism, duplicates, and quality issues. Using advanced pattern recognition and language analysis techniques, the system performs large-scale comparisons across documents and flags anomalies for review. Integrated into the grant management workflow, it reduces manual checks and standardises evaluation. Processing time per document has been reduced from around five hours to 1.5 hours—an 80% reduction—enabling the handling of approximately 45,000 documents and up to one billion comparisons. The impact includes faster payments, improved traceability, reduced fraud risk, and significant cost savings. The programme benefits from scalable, secure processing, while SMEs receive quicker support, strengthening trust and efficiency in public funding delivery.

column2: City of Valencia chatbot for citizens on tax enquiries
column4: Operating on the millie platform from 1MillionBot, Gestri offers seamless integration into various communication channels, providing instant responses in Valencian, Spanish and English. This platform allows complete data management and personalisation of the assistant, ensuring that each interaction is effective and adapted to the user's needs. The constant availability of Gestri, which operates 24 hours a day through the Provincial Council web portal and other digital media, has optimised taxpayer service. This has allowed the Valencia Provincial Council to free up resources for tasks that require a more human and specialised approach.
column11: Executive and legislative organs, financial and fiscal affairs, external affairs.

column2: Valencia Provincial Council – Gestri Tax Enquiries Chatbot
column4: Rental subsidy programmes are critical social policies but are often slowed by manual document verification and repeated requests for information. The Valencian Regional Government developed a digital solution to streamline these procedures. This use case involves applicants, housing authorities, and administrative staff, with the objective of reducing processing times and administrative burden while improving service quality. The solution automatically verifies rental contracts and payslips submitted by applicants, cross-checking data to avoid redundant documentation requests. Integrated into the subsidy application workflow, it enables straight-through processing for standard cases and flags only complex or ambiguous applications for manual review. The impact includes significantly shorter verification times, faster decisions for applicants, and more efficient use of staff resources. Administrative personnel can concentrate on cases requiring human judgement, improving overall programme effectiveness. The solution enhances scalability, consistency, and fairness in subsidy delivery, strengthening public trust and ensuring timely support for households in need.
column42: Automated reasoning and Automated Decision Making (ADM)

column2: Recognition of university degrees with AI
column4: Lengthy recognition processes for foreign university degrees have historically delayed labour market integration for qualified professionals in Spain. The Ministry of Science, Innovation, and Universities sought to modernise this process to address skills shortages and administrative inefficiencies. This use case involves applicants, universities, professional bodies, and ministry staff, with the objective of reducing waiting times and simplifying procedures. The solution automates document handling, validation, and comparison against national qualification frameworks, eliminating repetitive paperwork and manual checks. Integrated into ministerial systems, it standardises workflows and accelerates decision-making. The impact is a reduction in recognition timelines from years to months, enabling faster entry of foreign professionals into high-value sectors. The ministry benefits from increased efficiency and policy effectiveness, while applicants experience clearer, faster outcomes. The solution supports labour market mobility, improves public administration performance, and contributes to economic competitiveness.
column8: Ministry of Science, Innovation, and Universities
column42: Automated reasoning and Automated Decision Making (ADM)

column2: AI for Fiscal Compliance
column4: Tax administrations face growing complexity in managing large volumes of data while ensuring compliance, fairness and efficiency. The Spanish Tax Agency aims to modernise tax and customs operations by shifting from reactive enforcement to preventive and supportive compliance models. The objective of this initiative is to simplify taxpayer obligations, improve data accuracy and reduce unnecessary audits. The implementation applies analytical models to identify common reporting errors, inconsistencies and risk patterns in declarations and customs data. These tools are embedded within a human-in-the-loop framework, where tax professionals continuously supervise, validate and refine system outputs. The approach prioritises transparency, accountability and support for taxpayers rather than automated enforcement. As the system is still in development, testing focuses on reliability, explainability and operational integration. Expected impacts include fewer errors in tax filings, reduced administrative burden for both taxpayers and staff, and more targeted audit activities. Qualitative benefits include improved trust in tax administration, better allocation of inspection resources and enhanced effectiveness of fiscal governance.

column2: BRAVA (Bid Rigging Algorithm for Vigilance in Antitrust)
column4: Public procurement is vulnerable to collusion and bid rigging, which undermine competition and waste public funds. Spain’s competition authority, CNMC, developed BRAVA to strengthen oversight of procurement markets. The use case involves procurement authorities, competition regulators, and public bodies, with the objective of identifying anti-competitive behaviour more effectively. BRAVA analyses large datasets from public tenders, examining bidding patterns, pricing behaviours, and relationships between participants. It classifies tenders as competitive or potentially collusive, supporting investigators with risk-based prioritisation. Integrated into CNMC analytical workflows, the tool enhances early detection and evidence gathering. The impact includes improved enforcement capability, more efficient investigations, and stronger deterrence of anti-competitive practices. Recognised with a national award, BRAVA delivers significant public value by protecting competition, improving market integrity, and safeguarding public spending.
column8: Comisión Nacional de los Mercados y la Competencia (CNMC)

column2: HipatIA - AI-driven forecasting tool for educational services
column4: Education authorities must anticipate changing student needs to allocate teachers, classrooms, and training programmes effectively. The Andalusian Regional Government introduced HipatIA to support proactive planning. This use case involves education departments, schools, and policymakers, with the objective of improving responsiveness and educational outcomes. HipatIA analyses historical student data to forecast key indicators such as enrolment trends, dropout risks, and demand for vocational training. Integrated into education planning systems, it supports evidence-based decisions on resource allocation and early interventions. The impact includes better anticipation of staffing and infrastructure needs, improved student support through targeted tutoring and guidance, and more efficient use of public resources. Education authorities gain forward-looking insights, while schools benefit from improved planning certainty. The solution enhances service quality, equity, and sustainability in the education system.
column8: Ministry of Education and Vocational Training, Regional Government of Andalusia

column2: Smart lighting with AI in Barcelona
column4: Urban congestion contributes significantly to travel delays, emissions and reduced quality of life in large cities. In Barcelona, traffic management required more adaptive solutions to respond to real-time conditions. The objective of this pilot is to reduce congestion and emissions by optimising traffic light operations. The implementation deploys sensor-equipped traffic lights that collect real-time traffic flow data and dynamically adjust signal timings. Analytical models assess traffic density and optimise phase durations to improve throughput during peak hours. The system integrates with the city’s broader mobility infrastructure under the Sustainable Mobility Plan. The pilot aims to reduce congestion by up to 20%, shorten travel times and lower carbon emissions. Qualitative impacts include smoother traffic flows, improved air quality and a scalable framework for intelligent urban traffic management.

column2: ADIA - the AI Document Assistant for spanish Courts
column4: Judicial systems handle vast volumes of documents containing sensitive personal data, making anonymisation and summarisation time-consuming but essential. Spain’s Ministry of Justice introduced ADIA to improve efficiency and data protection. This use case involves judges, clerks, legal professionals, and court administrators, with the objective of streamlining document handling while ensuring confidentiality. ADIA is implemented as a secure digital service accessible through internal judicial networks. It automatically removes personal identifiers from documents and generates concise summaries highlighting key information. Integrated with existing court systems, it supports nationwide use through a web interface. The impact includes faster document preparation, reduced manual effort, improved compliance with data protection requirements, and enhanced productivity for legal professionals. ADIA delivers measurable efficiency gains, strengthens privacy safeguards, and supports a more modern, responsive justice system.
column8: Spanish Ministry of the Presidency, Justice and Parliamentary Relation

column2: Canal Empresa - reducing administrative burden and providing regulatory information for businesses
column4: Businesses and self-employed professionals often face fragmented administrative procedures, duplicated requests for information, and difficulty understanding regulatory obligations across different public bodies. The Generalitat Valenciana launched Canal Empresa to reduce administrative burden and improve the relationship between the business community and public administration. The platform involves regional authorities, public-sector entities, companies, and freelancers, with the objective of centralising procedures, guidance, and regulatory information in a single digital access point. Canal Empresa is implemented as an integrated digital platform that consolidates information and services from the Generalitat and its public-sector organisations, covering the full business lifecycle from start-up to daily operations. A built-in virtual assistant provides immediate guidance, helping users navigate procedures, identify applicable regulations, and access the correct forms and services. The platform integrates with existing administrative back-office systems to ensure consistency and up-to-date content. The impact includes faster access to information, fewer errors in submissions, and reduced need for in-person or telephone support. Businesses benefit from clearer processes and time savings, while the administration gains efficiency, improved service quality, and better compliance outcomes, strengthening the region’s overall business environment.

column2: PREDUCA - Student learning pathways personalisation
column4: Public education systems must address diverse student needs while planning resources efficiently at scale. The Andalusian Government developed PREDUCA to personalise learning pathways and support strategic planning in public education. This use case involves students, teachers, school leaders, and education authorities, with the objective of identifying individual learning needs early and enabling tailored educational interventions. PREDUCA builds on the HipatIA system and is integrated into the regional school management platform. It analyses historical academic performance, attendance, and behavioural data to detect patterns and predict future learning needs. Based on these insights, the system generates recommendations for targeted interventions, such as early support for at-risk students or enriched activities for high achievers. Educators use these insights to adapt lesson plans, materials, and classroom strategies. The impact includes more timely and personalised support for students, improved educational outcomes, and better use of teaching resources. Education authorities gain predictive insights for planning staffing and programmes, while schools benefit from data-informed decision-making, contributing to a more equitable and effective education system.

column2: ESKOLA ADIMENTSUA - Automation, robotics, and artificial intelligence in teaching
column4: Education systems must prepare students and teachers for a society shaped by automation, robotics, and advanced digital technologies. The Basque Government launched Eskola Adimentsua for the 2024–25 academic year to embed these competencies into teaching and learning. The initiative involves schools, teachers, students, and education authorities, with the objective of promoting active learning and strengthening digital skills across the education system. Eskola Adimentsua is implemented as a comprehensive programme combining curriculum innovation with sustained teacher support. A core component is professional development, offering mentoring, peer collaboration, and continuous guidance to help educators integrate computational thinking, programming, and digital tools into everyday teaching. Teachers are supported throughout the entire process, from design to classroom implementation. The impact includes increased teacher confidence, improved digital literacy among students, and stronger alignment between education and future labour market needs. By empowering educators as innovation leaders, the programme delivers long-term value, fostering resilient, future-ready schools and positioning education as a driver of digital transformation.

column2: AI to anticipate peaks in Hospital La Fe (Valencia)
column4: Large hospitals face unpredictable fluctuations in emergency department demand, which can strain staff, facilities, and patient care. Hospital Universitari i Politècnic La Fe in Valencia sought to improve its ability to anticipate peaks in pressure within its Adult Emergency Department. This use case involves hospital management, clinicians, and operational teams, with the objective of enabling proactive resource planning and improving patient flow. The solution combines anonymised historical patient data with real-time operational information to estimate the likelihood of hospital admission for incoming patients. Key factors such as age, triage priority, prior conditions, previous admissions, and diagnostic test results are analysed to support early decision-making. Integrated into hospital information systems, the tool provides staff with actionable insights to anticipate surges and adjust staffing, beds, and workflows accordingly. The impact includes improved preparedness, reduced overcrowding, and more efficient use of clinical resources. Patients benefit from smoother care pathways, while the hospital improves service quality, resilience, and operational efficiency under pressure.
column8: La Fe Hospital - Valencian Regional Government

column2: LOLA Chatbot for healthcare services
column4: Healthcare systems face growing demand for chronic care and elderly monitoring, while resources remain constrained. LOLA addresses this challenge by extending clinical monitoring into patients’ homes. This use case involves hospitals, clinicians, chronically ill and elderly patients, and health authorities, with the objective of improving continuity of care and reducing unnecessary in-person interactions. LOLA is implemented as a cloud-based virtual healthcare assistant that conducts automated medical phone conversations according to predefined clinical protocols. It integrates with hospital information systems and allows clinicians to configure patient cohorts, monitoring schedules, and escalation rules. The platform can perform tens of thousands of calls per day without local installation, enabling rapid adoption across regions and specialties. The impact includes earlier detection of health issues, reduced workload for healthcare professionals, and improved patient experience through regular, structured follow-up. Hospitals benefit from scalable, cost-effective monitoring, while patients receive personalised attention at home, contributing to better outcomes and more sustainable healthcare delivery.
column8: Spanish hospitals (e.g. La Fe, among others)

column2: ARI (Intelligent Robotic Assistant)
column4: Organisations in sectors such as transport, energy and finance face complex optimisation problems that are difficult to solve with conventional computing methods. In Galicia, strategic industries required innovative approaches to manage large-scale combinatorial challenges. The objective of this pilot is to explore advanced optimisation techniques for real-world business scenarios. The implementation investigates quantum-inspired and quantum computing methods to address routing, scheduling and resource allocation problems. Collaborative experimentation between the Galician Supercomputer Centre and industry experts enables testing of algorithms on realistic datasets and constraints. The pilot provided insights into performance improvements, feasibility and future applicability of quantum approaches. Qualitative impacts include enhanced optimisation capabilities, strengthened regional innovation ecosystems and a clearer pathway for integrating next-generation computing into business decision-making.

column2: PALOMA - Madrid’s AI-powered virtual assistant
column4: Unwanted loneliness among elderly people is a growing social challenge with significant health and wellbeing implications. The City of Madrid introduced PALOMA to proactively identify and support older residents at risk of social isolation. This use case involves social services, elderly citizens, and care providers, with the objective of detecting vulnerability early and connecting individuals to appropriate support. PALOMA systematically contacts residents aged over 75 who live alone, initiating personalised phone conversations based on a short wellbeing questionnaire. The process respects privacy and data protection and maintains human oversight, ensuring that social workers remain involved in assessment and follow-up. When risk indicators are detected, PALOMA triggers referrals to municipal services or community support. The impact includes earlier identification of loneliness, more targeted interventions, and better allocation of social worker time. The initiative improves quality of life for older residents, strengthens preventive social care, and supports a more humane and efficient response to an increasingly pressing societal issue.

column2: ADMAIES Project (Improving administrative decision-making through the use of AI, experimentation, and sandboxes)
column4: Public administrations increasingly rely on complex decision-making processes that must balance efficiency, transparency and legal certainty. However, limited experimentation spaces and unclear governance frameworks often slow responsible innovation. The objective of the ADMAIES project is to explore how experimental approaches, including regulatory sandboxes, can support the responsible integration of analytical technologies into administrative decision-making. The implementation is led by academic researchers and focuses on designing, testing and evaluating decision-support tools within controlled environments. These experiments examine how such tools interact with legal frameworks, organisational practices and ethical requirements. Rather than deploying operational systems, the project generates evidence, methodologies and governance models that public administrations can adopt. Expected impacts include improved understanding of risks and benefits, clearer guidelines for responsible experimentation and stronger institutional capacity to innovate. Qualitative outcomes include enhanced transparency, better alignment between technology and administrative law, and a reusable framework for safely testing digital solutions in the public sector.

column2: Streamlining talent acquisition and verification with RPA (Robotic Process Automation)
column4: Public-sector recruitment processes often require extensive manual verification of candidates’ qualifications, creating delays and heavy workloads. The Andalusian Health Service introduced robotic process automation to streamline verification of certificates and documents submitted during selection processes. This use case involves recruitment panels, administrative staff, and job candidates, with the objective of accelerating evaluations while maintaining fairness and accuracy. The solution automates repetitive verification tasks, such as checking academic qualifications, professional experience, and training records against defined criteria. Integrated into recruitment workflows, the system processes large volumes of documentation consistently and efficiently. Since its launch in July 2022, approximately 14,000 merits have been verified. The impact includes significantly reduced processing times, improved consistency in scoring, and lower administrative burden. Staff can focus on complex assessments and decision-making, while candidates benefit from faster, more transparent recruitment processes, improving trust and efficiency in public employment systems.
column42: Robotics and Automation and Robotic Process Automation (RPA)

column2: Optimising businesses: Unlocking the potential of Quantum Computing
column4: Many strategic sectors, including transport, logistics, energy and finance, face complex optimisation problems where the number of possible solutions grows exponentially, making traditional approaches inefficient or impractical. In Galicia, organisations struggled to optimise routes, schedules and resource allocation while respecting multiple operational constraints. The objective of this pilot is to explore advanced optimisation approaches that can deliver better solutions to real-world business problems. The implementation was led by the Galician Supercomputer Centre in collaboration with Ayesa and focused on applying quantum and quantum-inspired algorithms to combinatorial optimisation scenarios. Representative industrial problems were modelled mathematically and tested using emerging quantum programming techniques and high-performance computing environments. The pilot evaluated feasibility, performance gains and integration pathways with existing decision-support systems. Results showed improved solution quality for selected optimisation problems and provided insights into where quantum approaches can complement classical methods. Qualitative impacts include strengthened innovation capacity, increased readiness of regional industries to adopt next-generation computing, and clearer pathways towards future commercial deployment as quantum technologies mature.

column2: Q4Real: Quantum Computing for real industries
column4: Industrial companies often face shared optimisation and simulation challenges that limit efficiency, sustainability and competitiveness, yet lack practical frameworks to adopt emerging computational approaches. The Basque Government identified the need to accelerate adoption of advanced solutions across the industrial sector. The objective of this pilot is to bridge the gap between experimental quantum technologies and real industrial applications with sustainable impact. The implementation established a collaborative framework bringing together industry, research and technology providers to standardise quantum programming practices. The project developed reusable components, reference architectures and a shared knowledge base for different classes of quantum problems, alongside emerging quantum software engineering methodologies. These elements were tested on representative industrial use cases to assess feasibility and performance. The pilot strengthened technical capacity, reduced barriers to entry and aligned innovation efforts with market needs. Qualitative impacts include improved industrial readiness, shared standards for future development and a clearer route to commercialisation of quantum-enabled solutions, supporting long-term digital leadership.

column2: 5G pilot in Andalusia for the María Zambrano Train Station
column4: Large transport hubs require efficient passenger navigation and proactive infrastructure maintenance to ensure safety, comfort and operational continuity. At María Zambrano train station, managing complex spatial layouts and real-time operational events posed challenges for both travellers and maintenance teams. The objective of this pilot is to enhance passenger experience and asset management through immersive digital tools. The implementation created a detailed digital twin of the station using Building Information Modelling, hosted in a cloud environment and connected via 5G. Two augmented reality applications were developed: a passenger guidance tool providing real-time navigation and points of interest, and a maintenance application supporting preventive and corrective tasks through visual overlays. The high-speed connectivity ensured immediate synchronisation of real-time data, including crowd levels and incidents. The pilot improved navigation efficiency, reduced maintenance response times and enhanced situational awareness. Qualitative benefits include improved service quality, safer operations and a scalable model for smart transport infrastructure.
column8: Ministry of Economic Affairs and Red.es

column2: Margot AI supporting the Agenzia Italiana del Farmaco - document search case
column4: Public agencies managing complex regulatory domains often struggle to retrieve precise information quickly from large internal document repositories. The Italian Medicines Agency adopted Margot AI to support customer service and internal staff in accessing authoritative information. This use case involves AIFA staff, regulated stakeholders, and citizens, with the objective of improving response accuracy and efficiency. Margot AI is implemented using a retrieval-augmented generation architecture that connects natural language queries to internal databases and document collections. It searches, classifies, and retrieves relevant content, delivering precise answers while maintaining alignment with official sources. Integrated across existing systems and communication channels, it automates repetitive requests and supports staff in handling complex enquiries. The impact includes faster response times, reduced manual searching, and improved consistency of information provided. AIFA benefits from more efficient service delivery and better use of institutional knowledge, strengthening regulatory transparency and operational effectiveness.

column2: Margot AI supporting the Agenzia Italiana del Farmaco - FarmaciLine assistance
column4: Citizens frequently seek reliable information on authorised medicines, indications, and regulatory decisions, placing high demand on public health information services. Within AIFA’s FarmaciLine service, Margot AI supports responses to these enquiries. This use case involves citizens, healthcare professionals, and AIFA operators, with the objective of delivering accurate, timely information on medicines. The system retrieves information from official sources such as Summaries of Product Characteristics and AIFA resolutions, ensuring responses remain authoritative. Integrated into FarmaciLine workflows, it handles repetitive questions and supports multilingual, consistent communication. The impact includes improved accessibility to trusted medicines information, reduced workload for operators, and enhanced user satisfaction. Citizens benefit from clear, immediate answers, while AIFA strengthens its role as a reliable source of pharmaceutical information.

column2: Margot AI supporting the Agenzia Italiana del Farmaco - Monitoring platform
column4: AIFA manages multiple monitoring registries used to track medicine use in clinical practice, which are complex and heterogeneous. Accessing the correct information quickly is essential for regulatory oversight and operational efficiency. This use case supports AIFA staff by simplifying interaction with monitoring registries. Margot AI retrieves relevant data and guidance from different registry systems, including therapeutic plans and multi-drug registries, through a unified conversational interface. Integrated with internal platforms, it automates information retrieval and supports staff decision-making. The impact includes reduced search time, improved accuracy, and better utilisation of monitoring data. This strengthens regulatory oversight, enhances operational efficiency, and supports evidence-based management of medicines within the Italian healthcare system.

column2: AI-powered procurement platform
column4: Public procurement processes are often time-consuming, error-prone and vulnerable to inefficiencies that undermine transparency and competition. In Albania, authorities seek to modernise procurement practices to improve fairness and operational efficiency. The objective of this initiative is to streamline procurement workflows while maintaining strong human oversight. The implementation is developing a platform that supports drafting terms of reference, estimating costs and generating initial evaluation reports. All outputs are subject to review, with full audit trails tracking modifications. The system integrates procurement data and standard templates to support consistent application of rules. Expected impacts include reduced processing time, fewer errors and increased transparency. Qualitative benefits include fairer competition, improved trust among businesses and stronger governance of public spending.

column2: AI for taxation project in Albania
column4: Combating tax evasion and financial crime requires timely detection of anomalies across large volumes of transactional data. Albania aims to enhance its tax administration by automating routine analysis while strengthening investigative capacity. The objective of this project is to improve detection of irregularities and free staff for complex cases. The implementation develops analytical models that process electronic invoices and customs declarations in near real time to identify unusual patterns. The system supports analysts rather than replacing them, integrating into existing workflows. Expected impacts include improved compliance rates, faster detection of risks and reduced manual workload. Qualitative outcomes include more effective enforcement, better use of human expertise and increased fiscal integrity.
column11: Executive and legislative organs, financial and fiscal affairs, external affairs.

column2: AI for healthcare project in Albania
column4: Healthcare systems face pressure to detect diseases earlier and manage growing diagnostic workloads. Albania aims to strengthen diagnostic capacity through advanced digital tools. The objective of this project is to support clinicians in radiology by improving early detection and treatment planning. The implementation focuses on developing systems that analyse medical images to identify patterns associated with disease, support personalised treatment decisions and predict progression. These tools are designed to integrate into clinical workflows and support, not replace, medical judgement. Expected impacts include earlier diagnosis, improved patient outcomes and reduced diagnostic delays. Qualitative benefits include improved care quality, increased system resilience and long-term efficiency gains.

column2: Nation-wide AI-powered smart city project
column4: Rapid urbanisation and increased mobility place pressure on city infrastructure, traffic management and emergency response. Albania has launched a nationwide initiative to modernise urban management across 20 cities. The objective of this project is to improve safety, mobility and service coordination through real-time data-driven management. The implementation develops an integrated platform that combines urban, traffic and public safety data into a central command and control environment. Analytical tools support traffic optimisation, incident detection and coordinated emergency response. Expected impacts include reduced congestion, improved road safety and faster response times. Qualitative outcomes include more resilient cities, better public service coordination and a scalable model for national smart city deployment.

column2: UCRONIA - Utility Circular Economy Real-Time Operation Navigation Integration Assets
column4: U.C.R.O.N.I.A. is a new smart system developed by AMA S.p.A. for managing urban hygiene services in Rome, unveiled at the Smart City World Congress in Barcelona. The name is an acronym for Utility, Circular Economy, Real-time, Operation, Navigation, Integration, and Assets, reflecting the project's goal to shift from reactive to preventive waste management. At the heart of the system is a sophisticated geographic digital twin that maps Rome’s entire urban infrastructure in real time—including manholes, waste bins, tree-lined streets, vehicle fleets, citizen reports, and environmental data. This data feeds into a dedicated control room, enabling strategic, data-driven decisions, optimized operations, and continuous improvement in service quality, with positive impacts on sustainability and the circular economy. Already operating 24/7, U.C.R.O.N.I.A. is managed by AMA in collaboration with high-tech partners like Present S.p.A. and Esri Italia, and has proven effective during major events such as papal funerals and Jubilee celebrations—positioning Rome as a leading example of a smart city.

column2: IMPULSE Project - NemID card retrieval from lockers (IMPULSE - Denmark)
column4: Access to digital public services in Denmark depends on NemID credentials, which vulnerable citizens frequently lose, limiting their ability to interact with authorities. Aarhus Municipality sought solutions to ensure equitable access for these groups. The objective of this pilot is to support vulnerable citizens by providing secure storage and retrieval of authentication credentials. The implementation introduced personal document lockers where NemID key cards could be safely stored. Access to each drawer is controlled through biometric facial recognition performed via the citizen’s mobile device, ensuring secure and exclusive access. The solution integrates with existing municipal support services and digital identity workflows. The pilot reduced credential loss, improved continuity of service access and increased digital inclusion. Qualitative impacts include enhanced autonomy for vulnerable users, reduced administrative burden on social services and improved trust in digital public services, demonstrating inclusive innovation in identity management.

column2: IMPULSE Project - Gijón public services app
column4: Secure and user-friendly electronic identity systems are essential for accessing modern public services, yet many citizens face fragmented authentication experiences. The City of Gijón aimed to modernise access to municipal services while ensuring privacy and interoperability. The objective of this pilot is to enhance secure, citizen-centric digital identification within the public sector. The implementation explored integration of advanced identity technologies into municipal platforms, focusing on GDPR-compliant authentication and cross-service usability. The pilot leveraged existing infrastructure to test secure access to public services and evaluate interoperability at local and cross-border levels. Results include improved user experience, stronger data protection and increased confidence in digital services. Qualitative benefits include enhanced accessibility, reduced administrative friction and alignment with European digital identity objectives, supporting scalable adoption across public administrations.

column2: IMPULSE Project - Entreprise digital drawer
column4: Businesses frequently face administrative inefficiencies when accessing public services, as they are required to repeatedly provide the same identity documents, registrations and certifications to different authorities, often across borders. This duplication increases costs, delays procedures and creates inconsistencies in data verification. Within the IMPULSE initiative, public administrations and business support organisations sought to simplify and secure enterprise access to digital public services while maintaining regulatory compliance. The objective of this pilot is to streamline business identification processes and enable trusted, reusable digital identities for enterprises. The implementation designed and tested a blockchain-based business identity solution, often referred to as a digital drawer, which enriches traditional business identities with verifiable attributes sourced from official business registers. These attributes can be securely shared with authorised public bodies on demand, with full traceability and user control. The system integrates with existing public service platforms and supports both domestic and cross-border service access. The pilot demonstrated faster onboarding to public services, reduced administrative burden for businesses and authorities, and improved trust in shared data. Qualitative impacts include higher data integrity, improved interoperability between administrations and a reusable framework that supports more efficient, transparent and scalable digital services for enterprises.
column16: Authentication of self-sovereign Digital ID services

column2: IMPULSE Project - Civil registration & certification in Bulgaria
column4: Local administrations are under increasing pressure to deliver digital public services that are secure, reliable and compliant with data protection regulations, while maintaining accessibility for citizens. In the Municipality of Peshtera, existing electronic civil registration and certification services required strengthening to meet growing security and GDPR compliance demands. The objective of this pilot is to modernise municipal digital services by enhancing trust, data protection and operational robustness. The implementation built upon the municipality’s existing electronic service platform, integrating identity verification, secure data handling mechanisms and governance principles developed within the IMPULSE project. These enhancements focused on improving authentication, protecting personal data and ensuring that digital interactions between citizens and the municipality complied with European data protection requirements. The pilot enabled the municipality to test and validate these mechanisms in real operational conditions. Results included improved service reliability, stronger protection of citizen data and increased trust in digital civil services. Qualitative benefits include reduced administrative risk, greater efficiency in service delivery and a scalable model that other municipalities can adopt to modernise local e-government services while ensuring compliance and citizen confidence.

column2: IMPULSE Project - Online complaints filing services
column4: Filing a formal police complaint traditionally requires citizens to attend a police station in person, which can be time-consuming, inconvenient and particularly challenging for vulnerable individuals or those living far from urban centres. In the Basque Country, the Ertzaintza sought to modernise its complaint-handling processes while maintaining high standards of security, legal validity and public trust. The objective of this pilot is to enable citizens to securely submit complaints online, reducing administrative barriers and improving access to public security services. The implementation integrated advanced identity management mechanisms into the existing online complaints system, enabling secure authentication and verification of complainants. These mechanisms were designed and tested in collaboration with law enforcement personnel, ensuring alignment with operational requirements and legal constraints. The pilot included end-user needs analysis, system validation and staff training to support adoption. Results showed improved accessibility of police services, reduced need for in-person visits and more efficient handling of non-urgent complaints. Qualitative impacts include improved citizen convenience, better allocation of police resources and strengthened trust in digital public safety services. The pilot demonstrates a scalable approach for modernising law enforcement interactions while preserving security and procedural integrity.

column2: BRIGHT - Local Energy Cooperative multi-market centralized aggregation for value stacking flexibility services (Belgium)
column4: De Nieuwe Dokken in Ghent, Belgium, was developed to include 400 housing units, an administrative building, a sports facility, and a kindergarten, along with extensive renewable energy and circular technology infrastructure operated by DuCoop ESCO. As part of a Horizon 2020 project, DuCoop and its partners implemented smart services—such as real-time energy consumption visualization, billing, and smart home features—to raise energy awareness and engage residents in the energy community. The initiative integrated data-driven tools, blockchain technology, and social science approaches to foster residential participation in demand response (DR) programs, using technologies like Digital Twin models, Virtual Power Plants, and AI-driven services to enhance grid flexibility. With resident collaboration, the pilot aimed to characterize consumer behavior to assess the potential for integrating the dwellings into a virtual power plant system.
column47: Consortium (research institutions, private sector and other PAs)

column2: BRIGHT - Virtual Community Decentralized Aggregation in Slovenia
column4: The energy transition increasingly requires active participation from consumers, yet many households lack the tools to engage effectively in local energy markets or optimise their energy use. In Slovenia, energy stakeholders sought solutions to empower consumers while maintaining security, transparency and system efficiency. The objective of this pilot is to enable peer-to-peer energy trading and integrated energy services through a secure, consumer-centric platform. The implementation combines edge-secure gateways, advanced metering infrastructure and digital twin models with a distributed transaction platform to support decentralised energy exchanges. Aggregate measurements are analysed to infer device behaviour without exposing individual consumption patterns, while users interact through a unified interface that integrates energy and non-energy services, including financial and safety-related applications. The pilot demonstrated improved consumer engagement, enhanced visibility of energy usage and new opportunities for local energy optimisation. Qualitative impacts include increased user awareness, support for decentralised energy markets and a replicable national model for virtual energy communities. The project illustrates how integrated digital infrastructures can support sustainable energy systems and active consumer participation.
