column2: Diia.AI: The World's First National AI Agent for Government Services
column4: On September 1, 2025, Ukraine became the first country in the world to introduce an AI assistant for government services via its Diia portal. Announced by Digital Transformation Minister Mykhailo Fedorov on Telegram, the AI tool is currently in open beta testing. Designed to enhance convenience and efficiency, it guides users through accessing public services, recommends the most suitable options based on their personal situations, and can even provide vital personal data—such as insurance records—within the chat interface.
column8: Ministry of Digital Transformation of Ukraine

column2: Digital Management Platform for Public Administrations Sedipualb@
column4: SEDIPUALB@ is a digital platform created by the Provincial Council of Albacete to enable fully digital administrative management for municipalities. Initially launched to serve local governments within the province, it provides a shared, open, and interoperable system that simplifies administrative processes and eliminates barriers such as licensing costs or dependence on political cycles. Delivered as a Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) solution, the platform is built on open technologies and deployed in the cloud, ensuring scalability, flexibility, and cost-efficiency. Albacete became the only province in Spain where all municipalities adopted a unified digital management platform. Since then, SEDIPUALB@ has expanded well beyond its original scope. Today, more than 300 public entities—including municipalities, consortia, and universities across seven autonomous regions—use the system. It supports over a million citizens and thousands of concurrent users daily. Governance is collaborative, with technical, legal, and administrative bodies advising on improvements, while costs are distributed among participants according to usage. This model ensures both sustainability and transparency, turning a local project into a national benchmark for digital public administration.
column8: Provincial Council of Albacete (Diputación de Albacete)

column2: AI for Species Protection - Detection of Illegal Online Wildlife Trade
column4: Germany’s Federal Agency for Nature Conservation, together with the AI Lab at the German Environment Agency, developed an AI-driven tool to detect illegal online trading of protected species, one of the fastest-growing environmental crimes worldwide. The system combines AI-powered image classification, rule-based text analysis, and tailored web scraping to scan thousands of online listings daily, identifying species even when disguised or misspelled. During a six-month pilot, the tool flagged over 50,000 protected animals from more than 1,250 CITES-listed species, transforming an unmanageable manual task into a scalable, data-driven process. It frees public servants from repetitive screening, allowing them to focus on high-risk cases, improving efficiency, and strengthening enforcement. Built entirely in-house with open-source technologies, the system ensures digital sovereignty, sustainability, and compliance with European interoperability standards. It has already inspired international interest, including from the Czech CITES authority, and provides a transferable model for EU-wide biodiversity protection.
column8: AI Lab at the German Environment Agency (UBA) and Federal Nature Conservation Agency

column2: Impulsa Autónomo: Intelligent Platform to Personalise Grants and Subsidies for the Self-Employed in Galicia through AI
column4: The Xunta de Galicia developed an intelligent portal and widget to simplify access to grants and subsidies for self-employed professionals. By consolidating dispersed information into a single platform, the project reduces complexity and fragmentation in accessing funding opportunities. Using Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)–based Generative AI, the system integrates official databases to provide personalised recommendations, outperforming static catalogues. It was designed for rapid deployment with iterative testing and direct user pilots, ensuring relevance and usability. Early results show search time reductions of more than 60% and over 100 hours of integrated specialised advisory services. BI dashboards track consultations, usage, and real-time satisfaction, while automated responses deliver cost savings for the administration. Transparency is reinforced through personalised publication of subsidies, automatic alerts, and two-way feedback channels that enhance citizen participation. Fully aligned with European interoperability principles and Spanish regulations, the platform strengthens government-to-citizen interaction, increases public participation, and provides a replicable model for digital public services.

column2: AI-Driven Market Surveillance to Safeguard Consumers in the Digital Single Market
column4: Launched in 2022 by the European Commission’s Directorate-General for Justice and Consumers (DG JUST), this project delivers an EU-wide platform that enables systematic surveillance of online marketplaces across all EU and EEA languages. By simulating human interaction to overcome obstacles like pop-ups, captchas, and hidden content, it ensures access to data that traditional crawlers cannot reach. The platform records dangerous products taken offline, fostering transparency, cross-border cooperation, and a shared view of enforcement progress. With the integration of large language models, the system significantly improves the precision of product identification, reducing false positives and enabling inspectors to focus on genuine risks. This shift accelerates enforcement cycles, supports Member States in handling the growing influx of imported parcels, and enhances consumer protection across the Single Market. Aligned with European interoperability principles and EU product-safety legislation, the platform connects seamlessly with the Safety Gate Rapid Alert System. It provides authorities with a cost-effective, centralised, and secure service that strengthens regulatory enforcement while generating valuable insights for future EU policy-making.

column2: The Responsive PIAO: An AI-Enabled Model for Proactive and Continuous Skill Development
column4: Launched by LepidaSCpA in collaboration with the Italian public administration, the project introduces a strategic, data-driven approach to staff training, aligning professional development directly with the organisational objectives set out in the Piano integrato di attività e organizzazione (PIAO). Using an iterative Plan-Do-Check-Act cycle, it optimises training investment by mapping identified needs to existing courses on national platforms, ensuring both efficiency and value for money. Generative AI models, employed under human supervision, supported the design and analysis of tools such as the “interests and perceived gaps questionnaire”, which improved communication between management and employees and fostered organisational trust. The pilot phase recorded high levels of user satisfaction, validating the methodology’s potential to shift training strategies from intuition to evidence-based planning. Fully aligned with the European Interoperability Framework, the project demonstrates legal, organisational, semantic, and technical interoperability. It enhances transparency by creating a structured channel for employee feedback and by making its standardised training course index publicly available, offering a replicable model for other administrations.

column2: GREEN-LOG Last-Mile Digital Control Tower: An AI-Driven Platform for Real-Time Optimisation and Sustainable Urban Logistics
column4: The GREEN-LOG Last-Mile Digital Control Tower (DCT), developed as part of the EU co-funded GREEN-LOG project, addresses the growing challenges of urban last-mile logistics driven by e-commerce expansion. The platform provides real-time monitoring of delivery assets, near real-time forecasting of transport demand and traffic conditions, and advanced data-driven analytics to enhance operational coordination among logistics service providers, city authorities, and other stakeholders. Leveraging AI-powered predictive models, dynamic multi-modal routing, and Logistics-as-a-Service (LaaS) Marketplace metrics, the system optimises fleet utilisation, reduces carbon emissions, and minimises urban congestion while improving delivery efficiency and reliability. Demonstrations across European cities, including Athens, Barcelona, and Flemish urban areas, have shown reductions in kilometres driven (-35%), travel time (-26%), and the number of stops (-12%), delivering both environmental and economic benefits. The DCT’s flexible, modular architecture enables deployment across diverse urban contexts, supports cross-modal routing, and integrates policy constraints and incentives set by local authorities. Its design aligns with the European Interoperability Framework, ensuring legal, semantic, organisational, and technical interoperability, GDPR compliance, and secure, transparent data sharing. Stakeholder engagement and continuous co-creation have driven usability and adoption, with user satisfaction reaching 90% in early 2026, demonstrating the platform’s effectiveness in supporting sustainable and efficient urban logistics.

column2: Decision Support System Supporting Intelligent Real-time Resource Allocation of Response Teams (RART) for wildfire suppression
column4: The RART tool, developed under the SILVANUS GreenDeal project by NETCOMPANY, is a Decision Support System designed to assist fire commanders in optimising wildfire response. By dynamically allocating available firefighting resources, including aerial and ground units, based on fire spread projections, population density, and resource availability, the system enhances situational awareness and enables more effective, timely interventions, reducing the impact on citizens, property, and the environment. The tool integrates open-source population data, AI-driven fire forecasting, and a multi-objective optimisation algorithm to guide resource deployment under complex, time-sensitive conditions. Field tests in October 2024 by the Region of Sterea Ellada, Greece, and simulations in France demonstrated high user satisfaction (>90%) and confirmed the system’s effectiveness in supporting decision-making under real-life constraints.
column8: Region of Central Greece (Perifereia Stereas Elladas), Greece

column2: AI-Driven Early Warning and Decision Support for Fraud Prevention in Quality-Labelled Food Supply Chains
column4: The ALLIANCE EWDSS, developed by Netcompany S.A. as part of the EU-funded ALLIANCE project, is an AI-powered system designed to detect and mitigate food fraud across complex supply chains. Focusing on sectors such as Feta Cheese, Organic Pasta, Organic Honey, and Fava Beans, the solution combines Artificial Intelligence, fuzzy logic, operations research, and multi-criteria decision analysis to provide early risk detection, transparent risk scoring, and actionable decision support for farmers, auditors, and regulators. The system employs domain-specific monitoring—including real-time pH and temperature data for feta cheese, anomaly detection for honey, and machine learning models for fava beans—to proactively identify potential fraud before it enters production. Developed through a co-creation approach, it ensures usability, transparency, and stakeholder engagement, enabling evidence-based decision-making, consistent risk management, and compliance with food safety and certification standards. By integrating human-in-the-loop decision support and standardised data models, ALLIANCE EWDSS fosters collaboration, accountability, and trust across the food supply chain, representing a significant advancement over conventional single-method detection systems.
column8: Bavarian State Office for Health and Food Safety (Bayerisches Landesamt für Gesundheit und Lebensmittelsicherheit – LGL)

column2: AI-Driven Harmonisation of Albanian Legislation with the EU Aquis
column4: The AI-assisted platform, developed by Microsoft in partnership with the Albanian Government, accelerates the transposition of EU legislation into Albanian law, enhancing both administrative efficiency (G2G) and public service quality (G2C, G2B). By combining AI-powered translation (ChatGPT-4 architecture) with automated compliance table generation, the system reduces manual effort, improves accuracy, and tracks thousands of EU measures and national acts requiring alignment. Implemented within the existing PIEA (European Integration and Membership Platform), the solution supports Albanian institutions without restructuring, providing real-time access to CELEX documents and ensuring full interoperability across legal, organisational, semantic, and technical levels. Phase 2 introduces AI-based legal analysis to detect overlaps and contradictions, helping Albania stay on track to meet its EU integration objectives by 2030.
column8: National Agency of Information Society, under the Prime Minister’s authority
column11: Executive and legislative organs, financial and fiscal affairs, external affairs.

column2: Archimede (Antifrode) - Evolution of Anti-Fraud Systems and Continuous Internal Auditing
column4: Archimede is an AI-driven solution developed by INAIL to detect and anticipate errors, evasions, and fraud (EEF), supporting effective risk prevention policies and highlighting process anomalies. During testing, the system demonstrated high accuracy: in six central audits, 100% of flagged cases were confirmed as anomalous, with 44% showing non-compliance in case handling. By integrating with INAIL’s auditing systems and the CRIBIS business information database, Archimede enables the identification of mismanagement and process irregularities, forming a core component of ongoing monitoring and auditing strategies. Built as a versatile, open-source on-premise solution, it ensures secure handling of confidential data and is fully adapted to INAIL’s organisational and analytical context, providing a competitive technological advantage.
column8: Italian National Institute for Insurance against Accidents at Work (INAIL)

column2: The Pre-University Management Information System (SMIP/PUMIS)
column4: The Smart Management Information Platform (SMIP) was introduced to modernise Albania’s education governance and overcome the inefficiencies of manual, paper-based processes, fragmented communication, and limited digital access. As a unified platform, SMIP brings together education, health, and administration in a way not previously available in the country. It currently integrates more than 407,000 students, 35,000 teachers, and 800,000 parents, providing real-time academic updates for families, digital reporting tools for teachers, and centralised dashboards for education authorities. The platform also supports wider public services, such as issuing over 10,000 digital vaccine cards via the national e-Albania portal, and promoting digital literacy, with more than 74,000 students trained in ICT skills through SmartLabs. By interoperating with national systems like e-Albania, the e-Vaccine system, and the NAIS Government Data Centre, SMIP improves efficiency, reduces bureaucracy, and strengthens transparency in education management. Aligned with the country’s Digital Agenda 2022–2026 and designed in line with EIF interoperability principles, SMIP enables evidence-based policy-making, enhances parental engagement, and lays the foundation for future regional cooperation, including cross-border diploma recognition.
column8: National Agency for Information Society (NAIS) – in cooperation with the Ministry of Education and Sports.

column2: Cruscotto infortuni - Cognitive Systems for Road Accidents
column4: The Road Accident Analysis Dashboard, developed by INAIL, strengthens workplace safety policies by providing structured data and AI-driven insights into incidents that significantly contribute to annual injury statistics. Designed for the Central Health Supervisory Authority, the tool enables the identification of trends, risk factors, and intervention areas, supporting more targeted prevention strategies. Based on Oracle Analytics Server, Cloudera, and IBM Watson, the platform integrates institutional records with predictive analytics to improve data accuracy and uncover previously unclassified accidents. It currently covers incidents from 2018 onwards and will expand to a ten-year overview, with updates every six months. Early adoption shows strong user engagement, high satisfaction, and recognised added value, making it a reliable foundation for evidence-based decision-making and future predictive planning.
column8: Italian National Institute for Insurance against Accidents at Work (INAIL)

column2: MyInail Chatbot - support tool for INAIL users
column4: The MyInail Chatbot provides users with guided, conversational support across INAIL’s wide range of services, from healthcare and financial benefits to prevention and training. Launched in June 2024, it improves accessibility to INAIL’s knowledge base while directing users to the most appropriate support channels when needed. Integrated into both the INAIL web portal and mobile app, the chatbot reduces contact centre calls, ensures secure and privacy-compliant interactions, and is continuously updated through user feedback. Early adoption shows strong satisfaction and no reported service complaints, confirming its role as a reliable first-level assistance and knowledge management tool.
column8: Italian National Institute for Insurance against Accidents at Work (INAIL)

column2: AI-powered Linguistic Mediator: A Phygital Solution for Inclusive and Accessible Public Services at INAIL
column4: To respond to the needs of an increasingly diverse workforce—20% of insured workers in Italy are foreign-born—INAIL has developed the Linguistic Mediator, an AI-powered tool that provides real-time voice and text interpretation at service counters. Integrated into INAIL’s “phygital” service model, it helps front-office staff, social workers, and doctors communicate effectively with non-Italian-speaking users, improving access to services while streamlining internal processes. Early pilots in territorial offices have shown strong results, with translation accuracy around 92% and clear efficiency gains for staff. Built on Microsoft Azure AI Services, the solution is modular, secure, and interoperable, with future extensions planned for document translation and Italian Sign Language (LIS).
column8: Italian National Institute for Insurance against Accidents at Work (INAIL)

column2: JuntaGPT: the internal GenAI based assistant for civil servants within the biggest regional administration in Europe.
column4: In June 2025, the Andalusian Regional Government launched JuntaGPT, an on-premises generative AI assistant for its internal personnel — over 240,000 civil servants — to help with document drafting, normative queries, summarising, email writing, and other administrative text work. Developed by the regional digital agency (ADA) and deployed on the Hércules supercomputer at CICA, the system is designed to ensure digital sovereignty, data protection, and that all information remains securely within the Junta’s environment. It allows users to upload documents, ask questions naturally, chain conversational queries, and access pre-loaded information by domain without always having to upload files. Early stages of deployment are underway; the tool is already partly active and intended to improve productivity, efficiency, and internal service quality by streamlining the drafting and information-retrieval tasks of public employees.

column2: On Record, a tool to help engage youth and other stakeholders in current decision-making processes.
column4: Portugal launched the “On Record” platform to address the democratic challenge of politically disengaged youth, despite their digital savviness. Developed by LabX/ARTE with support from the Portuguese Institute for Sport and Youth (IPDJ) and the Cascais Municipality, the project is part of the EU-funded initiative Improving Civic Participation through Emerging Technologies. On Record is a modular, AI-enabled deliberation and engagement platform embedded in Participa.gov, designed specifically to amplify youth voices (ages 14–30) in policymaking. It combines structured dialogue environments, gamified learning tools, AI-powered transcription and summarisation, and user tracking to ensure continuity across participation cycles. Pilots (2025) have shown its capacity to lower barriers to participation, foster inclusive debate, and enhance transparency by linking proposals to policy follow-up. With interoperability built into its architecture, GDPR compliance, and integration with national digital identity tools, the solution is both scalable within Portugal and transferable across Europe. By co-designing with youth and institutions, Portugal is creating a sustainable digital public good that strengthens trust, accountability, and democratic engagement, while setting the foundations for broader adoption beyond youth participation.
column8: Agency for State Technological Reform Agency (ARTE), Portuguese Institute for Sport and Youth (IPDJ) and the Cascais Municipality

column2: KIEZ - an Agentic AI Assistant for Development Cooperation
column4: KIEZ is an AI-powered assistant developed by the Datalab of the German Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ) to support public servants in processing large volumes of text and improving decision-making in the domain of economic cooperation. Built as a trustworthy chat interface, KIEZ integrates agentic tools, domain-specific prompt templates, document uploads, controlled internet search, and internal databases, while balancing usability with strict privacy, security, and vendor-independence measures. The platform enables tasks such as document summarisation, translation, and refinement, and operates on a cloud-native, GDPR-compliant infrastructure that supports multiple AI models, including retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). KIEZ has already demonstrated strong impact, with 44% of surveyed staff rating it “important” or “very important” to their work and 51% reporting improvements in efficiency and communication. With over 1.6 million tokens processed daily, KIEZ exemplifies a scalable, secure, and interoperable AI solution for modernising public administration workflows, funded by NextGenerationEU.
column8: German Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)

column2: Alhambra and Generalife Digital Twin Living Lab: Technology to Preserve Beauty.
column4: The Alhambra Living Lab is an initiative led by the Andalusian Digital Agency (ADA) to develop a comprehensive digital twin of the Alhambra and Generalife palaces in Granada. Announced by Antonio Sanz, Regional Minister for the Presidency, Interior, Social Dialogue, and Administrative Simplification, and supported by Patricia del Pozo, Regional Minister for Culture and Sport, and Marifrán Carazo, Mayor of Granada, the project seeks to transform how visitors experience the Nasrid complex while improving its conservation, accessibility, and sustainability. Funded with over €3.2 million (€3,226,546) through the Spain Living Lab initiative within the Territorial Networks of Technological Specialisation (RETECH) programme, the Alhambra Living Lab will run until June 2026. The project integrates advanced technologies, including 3D modeling, IoT sensors, AI, mixed reality, and blockchain, to create a virtual replica of the monuments. This digital twin will allow the testing of innovative solutions to manage visitor flows, monitor environmental and structural conditions, enhance cultural engagement, and support sustainable heritage preservation. The first use cases are expected to launch in the first quarter of 2025, providing actionable insights for both site management and visitors. By combining state-of-the-art digital technologies with real-world heritage conservation, the Alhambra Living Lab represents one of Europe’s most forward-thinking cultural digital twin projects, offering a model for balancing tourism, sustainability, and citizen engagement.

column2: ParticipApp - more inclusive, transparent, and effective citizen participation in government decision-making
column4: ParticipApp is a national digital platform designed to make citizen participation in government decision-making more inclusive, transparent, and effective. The platform allows contributions in all co-official languages of Spain, and supports multiple input types, including text, voice, and images of handwritten notes. Using AI-powered tools, ParticipApp clusters and summarizes contributions thematically, helping participants and policymakers make sense of large volumes of input. The platform also provides AI-assisted drafting, enabling users to refine proposals while retaining full control over their content. Highly supported ideas can then be routed to a voting phase and formally considered in policy processes. For administrators, ParticipApp offers comprehensive dashboards to monitor participation, track emerging trends, moderate contributions, and measure engagement and impact. Built on open-source frameworks like Decidim and Consul and hosted on GDPR-compliant EU infrastructure, the platform is modular, scalable, and interoperable with existing digital public services. ParticipApp lowers barriers to participation, particularly for marginalised groups or those with limited digital literacy, and ensures transparency by providing visible summaries of contributions and clear feedback on how ideas are integrated into decision-making. By combining AI with human oversight, the platform strengthens public trust, enhances the quality and legitimacy of consultations, and establishes a sustainable digital public infrastructure for participatory governance, with potential for adoption across other European countries.
column8: Ministry for Digital Transformation and the Civil Service

column2: Integration of an Innovative Product – AI Technology – into Public Procurement Object Price Analysis and Forecasting, and Centralized Public Procurement Savings Identification Procedures
column4: The Republic of Lithuania is pioneering the integration of artificial intelligence (AI) into centralised public procurement to improve efficiency, transparency, and cost-effectiveness. Led by CPO LT, the national Central Purchasing Body, this project uses a generative AI solution — including large language models (LLMs) and advanced analytics — to analyse procurement prices, forecast costs, and calculate actual savings. In 2024, CPO LT managed €1.245 billion in procurements, representing around 15% of Lithuania’s total public procurement.The AI tool enables real-time market price analysis, identifies overpriced offers, predicts procurement values with high accuracy, and optimises supplier selection. These capabilities result in lower prices, reduced resource requirements for contracting authorities, increased supplier competition, and greater trust in procurement processes. The project complies with EU interoperability and procurement regulations, aligns with the European AI Act, and integrates open data sources such as CPO LT’s catalogue and kainos24.lt. Developed by NFQ Technologies, it is the first AI-based public procurement solution in Lithuania and represents a scalable model for other EU Member States. The pilot version is currently undergoing testing, with full deployment expected in December 2025, marking a significant step towards more transparent and efficient public procurement in the country.
column8: Public Institution CPO LT (Viešoji įstaiga CPO LT)

column2: Once-Only Common Services: Portugal uses cloud native, cloud agnostic building blocks to make accessing the Single Market simple and easier for EU citizens and businesses
column4: Portugal is making it easier for citizens to live, study, work, or retire in other EU countries through the Once-Only Technical System (OOTS), an EU-wide framework that reduces the administrative burden of cross-border procedures. With OOTS, citizens and businesses only need to submit required documents (“evidences”) once, as these can be securely exchanged between public authorities across Member States, including permits, certificates, diplomas, or licenses. In December 2023, Portugal went live with the OOTS Common Services, which enable interoperability between procedure portals across the EU, covering around 80,000 national competent authorities. The system reuses existing technical components like eID and eDelivery, and is developed as open-source, ensuring scalable, secure, and GDPR-compliant operations. OOTS simplifies life events such as registering a vehicle abroad, studying, or running a business, while improving administrative efficiency, trust in shared data, and transparency. Early studies suggest that SMEs and self-employed workers could save up to 50% of costs and time when completing cross-border procedures. Students applying to study abroad can also save days or weeks in administrative processes. Portugal is now progressing to level 5 on the OOTS Acceleratormeter, aiming for the first live transactions in collaboration with the Directorate-General for Higher Education. Through OOTS, Portugal contributes to a seamless EU digital single market, enabling faster, more transparent, and citizen-centric public services across Europe.

column2: (AILUME) - Artificial Intelligence-Based Application for the Early Detection of Forest Fire Outbreaks
column4: AILUME is an artificial intelligence system developed to strengthen Galicia’s response to one of its greatest environmental threats: forest fires. The region’s vast forest coverage, rural depopulation, and increasing climate change impacts make early detection essential. AILUME addresses this by continuously analysing video streams from the Xunta de Galicia’s forest surveillance camera network, strategically placed across the territory. Using advanced deep learning models, optimised YOLO detectors, and vision-language reasoning, the system identifies smoke, filters false alarms, and provides explainable alerts to operators at the Central Coordination Center for Forest Fire Defense. The platform combines state-of-the-art AI with robust software architecture: a Django-based modular backend, Apache Kafka for distributed real-time processing, PyAV/OpenCV for video capture, and PostgreSQL for structured data management. It includes a human-in-the-loop feedback system, ensuring that operator validation contributes to continuous learning and higher detection accuracy. Designed for resilience and scalability, the solution can handle dozens of concurrent video streams with sub-second data latency, operating 24/7 under real production conditions. Pilot testing launched in August 2025 has confirmed its effectiveness: 86.1% detection success in real-world cases, precision of 98.2%, recall of 99.9%, and detection lead times averaging 18 minutes earlier than official notification channels. The system has already proven especially valuable in depopulated rural areas where traditional surveillance is limited, outperforming commercial alternatives in both accuracy and speed.
column8: Agency for Technological Modernization of Galicia (AMTEGA) – Xunta de Galicia

column2: NORMA - virtual assistant for the analysis of legislative production
column4: Norma is a prototype developed from the Legislab proposal by the Polytechnic University of Milan and the Einaudi Institute for Economics and Finance. It uses Artificial Intelligence to interact with the Chamber of Deputies’ Legislative Observatory through natural language, making it easier to access data on the legislative process. For example, users can retrieve information on amendments, legislative texts, and the parliamentary progress of laws—data also used for the Chamber’s annual legislation report. Norma can also present results in the form of charts and tables, enhancing clarity and accessibility. By querying certified parliamentary archives, the system ensures accuracy and allows for reliable verification of legislative documentation. This innovation is expected to improve AI research capabilities and the generation of both statistical and analytical data on lawmaking, ultimately serving as a valuable tool for the Chamber’s institutional work.
column8: Polytechnic University of Milan and the Einaudi Institute for Economics and Finance

column2: MSE - assisted text system for parliamentary amendments
column4: MSE (Macchina Scrittura Emendamenti) is an AI-powered, assisted drafting system designed to support deputies in preparing parliamentary amendments. Developed by the inter-university consortium Alma Human AI (involving the Universities of Bologna, LUISS, CNR, University of Verona, and University of Turin), MSE offers a text-editor interface that operates directly on the draft bills under consideration. It transforms modifications into formally correct amendment proposals in real time, conforming to the Chamber’s strict syntactic rules. Additionally, MSE includes an internal chatbot accessible by commissions and parliamentary groups. This chatbot retrieves relevant documentation—such as dossiers, hearing transcripts, and deposited memoranda—and provides links to official sources. A future module (currently in development) will further assist by suggesting refined technical phrasing for amendments, guided by natural-language interaction with the user. Currently in use by parliamentary groups on a trial basis, MSE is expected to be officially rolled out by the end of 2025

column2: DEPUCHAT - chatbot to facilitate citizens' access to MPs' activities
column4: DEPUCHAT is an experimental project developed from an idea presented by Roma Tre University and the University of Florence, in response to a call launched by the Italian Chamber of Deputies. It is an advanced generative AI-based chatbot designed to provide citizens with fast, certified, and transparent information about the activities of Members of Parliament. Using official parliamentary data — including personal details, roles, legislative proposals, oversight acts, speeches, and committee work — DEPUCHAT allows users to search by topic, by individual deputy, or by specific issues (e.g., laws proposed on artificial intelligence). While strictly limited to parliamentary activity (excluding personal questions), the system aims to foster transparency, citizen participation, and data reliability. Currently in testing, it will undergo further data integration, stress testing, and security checks before being made publicly available as a valuable tool for civic engagement.
column8: Roma Tre University and the University of Florence

column2: Lab.ai: the heart of artificial intelligence in public administration
column4: Lab.ai is an initiative launched by Informatica Alto Adige (SIAG) under the strategy of introducing artificial intelligence into public administration. The project operates out of NOI Techpark in Bolzano and is responsible for coordinating the research, development, and training efforts related to AI applications in both public sector and local businesses. It was established as part of a structured AI strategy, including partnerships with Microsoft and Eurac Research, and will be supported by a dedicated AI competence center to ensure strategic oversight, ethical compliance, and innovation.

column2: Digital Twin: a digital replica for efficient management
column4: The Digital Twin project aims to digitally replicate entities such as users or territories to enhance inclusive and data-driven public services. Through this initiative, SIAG plans to enable more efficient service delivery by creating virtual models that mirror real systems. This concept aligns with broader efforts to make public administration more transparent, responsive, and inclusive—leveraging digital twins of citizens or geographic areas for advanced monitoring and decision-making.

column2: Automated bus lane and bus stop enforcement pilot with Hayden AI
column4: Barcelona has launched a six-month pilot project using artificial intelligence to enhance bus circulation by automatically monitoring and detecting obstructions in reserved bus lanes and at bus stops. Deployed on four buses running along the H12 and D20 routes, the system is supplied by Hayden AI and operates via cameras mounted inside the front of the vehicles. These cameras use AI-powered computer vision to capture images and video of static violations—such as unauthorized vehicles stopping or parking in bus lanes or at stops—that hinder regular bus service. Importantly, the system does not record license plates or people, and no fines are issued during the trial phase. Instead, it collects statistical and contextual data to help TMB (Transports Metropolitans de Barcelona) assess whether a larger rollout could be implemented in the future, potentially including enforcement actions. This is the largest deployment-to-date of bus-mounted automated enforcement cameras in Europe.

column2: Decidim - digital and democratic platform for citizen participation
column4: Decidim.Barcelona is an open-source digital platform developed by Barcelona City Council to foster inclusive citizen participation in municipal decision-making. It enables residents to engage in debates, submit proposals, and vote on various initiatives, thereby influencing the city's development. The platform integrates both digital and physical participation methods, ensuring accessibility and transparency in governance processes. Built on open-source software, Decidim.Barcelona allows for continuous improvement and adaptation, promoting democratic engagement and civic empowerment.

column2: PlatOne - PLATform for Operation of distribution Networks (Italian Pilot)
column4: PlatOne Project is a European initiative led by Areti, part of the Acea Group managing Rome’s electricity grid. Launched in 2019, its Italian pilot ran from September 2021 to August 2023. The project aimed to create a local flexibility energy market in the Rome metropolitan area, supporting reliable electricity supply amid growing renewable energy use and electrification. It involved 12 international partners and tested solutions like cogenerators, residential renewable energy systems with storage, smart office energy management, and EV charging infrastructure. By equipping the grid with advanced automation and monitoring, PlatOne enabled more efficient energy demand and supply management. The project concluded on June 28, 2023, showcasing its contributions to smart, flexible energy networks and citizen participation in energy management.
column47: Consortium (research institutions, private sector and other PAs)

column2: PlatOne - PLATform for Operation of distribution Networks (Germany Pilot)
column4: The PlatOne Reference Implementation is a laboratory-based development, part of the broader PlatOne Horizon 2020 project, led by RWTH Aachen University and Engineering Ingegneria Informatica S.p.A.. The aim of the PlatOne project is to create a scalable and secure framework for local flexibility markets, enabling Distribution System Operators (DSOs) to manage distributed energy resources more efficiently and support the integration of renewable energy. The Reference Implementation serves as the foundational prototype to test and validate the PlatOne Open Framework components before deployment in real-world pilots across Europe. Within this setup, core elements of the PlatOne framework, including the Blockchain Access Layer (BAL) and the DSO Technical Platform (DSOTP), were connected with a real-time power system simulator and a prototype Synchronized Measurement Unit (SMU). The laboratory environment also enabled testing and development of the Low-Cost Phasor Measurement Unit (PMU), improving its Technology Readiness Level, while data management and flexibility service tools were evaluated to ensure secure exchange and interoperability among components. The aim of the pilot is to demonstrate the PlatOne framework in real-world scenarios, validating its ability to coordinate distributed energy resources, enhance grid flexibility, and facilitate local energy markets, ultimately contributing to more reliable, efficient, and sustainable electricity distribution across participating regions.
column8: RWTH Achen University - Institute for Automation of Complex Power Systems

column2: Pioneering Use of Microsoft Copilot - Barnsley Council
column4: Barnsley Council has pioneered the use of Microsoft Copilot in local government. Starting in late 2023 with 300 licenses, the pilot quickly expanded to 2,000, and by January 2024 Copilot was rolled out across the organisation to automate administrative tasks and boost staff efficiency. The council emphasised stakeholder engagement, training, and governance, including an ethics board and protocols for high-risk areas like children’s social care. Early adoption has improved productivity in finance, business support, and social care, allowing staff to focus on more meaningful work while advancing digital transformation in public services.

column2: Foundry - Birmingham City
column4: Birmingham City Council launched the Foundry in 2023 to drive digital transformation, address budget challenges, and develop internal capabilities while reducing reliance on external consultants. Operating under "think big, start small, learn and scale quickly," it uses a collaborative, value-driven approach where multi-disciplinary teams co-develop AI solutions with frontline staff, ensuring practical interventions that improve efficiency, reduce costs, and enhance work experiences while fostering a digitally confident workforce committed to data-informed continuous improvement. The Foundry has implemented AI solutions across four critical back-office functions with measurable impact. Microsoft Copilot automates administrative tasks like legal document summaries and Subject Access Requests. A UiPath-powered automated redaction tool tackles overwhelming paperwork volumes from litigation claims, enabling timely compliance and reducing fine risks. A SharePoint translation tool handles simple documents in 30 languages, freeing translators for complex work. Most innovatively, an AI-powered communications mining solution analyzes emails for real-time citizen insights and automatically routes thousands of incoming messages to appropriate teams, transforming manual sorting into efficient automated processes that enable faster responses and improved citizen service.
column47: Consortium (research institutions, private sector and other PAs)

column2: Buckinghamshire Council: Early adoption of Microsoft Copilot
column4: Buckinghamshire Council became one of the first UK local authorities to adopt Microsoft Copilot in October 2023, granting early access to 300 staff across departments such as digital services, business support, and customer service. Their strategic rollout aimed to enhance staff productivity, improve work quality, achieve cost savings, mitigate risks, and promote staff well-being. To engage staff and identify practical use cases, the council hosted a "Dragon's Den"-style event where teams from various departments pitched how Copilot could benefit their work. This initiative not only generated excitement but also fostered cross-departmental collaboration. The council established a governance board, meeting fortnightly, to oversee the implementation, share progress, and address challenges. This proactive approach ensured that the adoption of Copilot was aligned with the council's strategic objectives and that any issues were promptly addressed.

column2: Greater Cambridge Shared Planning: AI-Driven Summarisation for Local Plan Consultations
column4: The Greater Cambridge Shared Planning (GCSP) service, a partnership between South Cambridgeshire District and Cambridge City councils, has integrated artificial intelligence (AI) into its planning consultation processes. Faced with the administrative challenge of processing approximately 9,500 public comments per Local Plan consultation, GCSP sought to reduce the time and resources required for summarising these responses. Through support from the Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government’s PropTech Innovation Fund, GCSP developed an AI tool capable of summarising consultation feedback in under an hour, compared to the previous 18.5 hours of officer time. This advancement allows planning officers to focus on higher-value tasks, such as analysing public feedback and engaging with stakeholders, thereby enhancing the efficiency and quality of the planning process

column2: The Family Hub in Halton Borough Council: Digital front door in family hub services
column4: Halton Borough Council has partnered with Beebot AI to develop a Digital Front Door for its Family Hub Services. This interactive platform provides 24/7 access to children’s and family services, offering personalised support through AI-driven chatbots and automated processes for tasks such as booking appointments. The initiative aims to improve accessibility and streamline service delivery for families.

column2: Hertfordshire County Council: AI road maintenance robot
column4: Hertfordshire County Council, in collaboration with Robotiz3d and the University of Liverpool, has developed an AI-powered robot to detect and repair road defects such as cracks that could become potholes. The robot autonomously identifies issues and carries out preventative repairs, improving road maintenance efficiency and reducing long-term costs.

column2: Kent County Council: Using predictive analytics to prevent homelessness in Maidstone and Kent
column4: Kent County Council leveraged predictive analytics to identify individuals and households at risk of homelessness in Maidstone and across Kent. By analysing data from multiple sources, the council can proactively intervene, provide targeted support, and allocate resources more effectively, aiming to reduce homelessness and improve community well-being.

column2: Lancashire County Council – A strategic approach to deploying AI in transport
column4: Lancashire County Council is adopting a strategic approach to deploying AI technologies in transport planning and management. The initiative focuses on using AI to optimise traffic flow, reduce congestion, and improve overall transportation infrastructure efficiency, contributing to smarter urban mobility solutions.

column2: Northumberland County Council- FloodAI, using AI to detect flash floods
column4: Northumberland County Council is undertaking a pilot project, 'FloodAI', an AI-driven system designed to monitor and detect flash floods in real-time. This project is funded by Defra as part of the £200 million Flood and Coastal Innovation Programmes which is managed by the Environment Agency. The programmes will drive innovation in flood and coastal resilience and adaptation to a changing climate. By analysing environmental data, the system provides early warnings, enabling timely responses to mitigate flood risks and enhance public safety.

column2: North Yorkshire Council: Using AI to reimagine Children’s Social Care
column4: North Yorkshire County Council is exploring the use of AI to transform children's social care services. Funded by the Department for Education's Data and Digital Solutions Fund, they developed a proof-of-concept project to reimagine case management using Artificial Intelligence (AI) powered by Microsoft Azure.  The project aims to leverage AI for better decision-making, resource allocation, and personalised support, ultimately improving outcomes for children and families in the region.

column2: Peterborough City Council: Hey Geraldine, a personalised AI assistant
column4: Peterborough City Council has introduced “Hey Geraldine,” a personalised AI assistant that provides residents with tailored information and guidance. The assistant uses AI to understand individual needs and preferences, enhancing resident engagement and satisfaction.

column2: South Cambridgeshire - Contact centre chatbot to improve resident satisfaction
column4: South Cambridgeshire District Council has deployed a chatbot in its contact centre to improve resident satisfaction. The AI-powered chatbot responds to common queries instantly, freeing staff to handle more complex issues and improving overall service efficiency.

column2: St Helens Borough Council – augmenting TEC with AI
column4: In 2024, St Helens Council launched its AI-driven Technology Enabled Care (TEC) Hub, a key component of its 2024-2027 Adult Social Care strategy and its "reablement – home first approach," to transform its services. This initiative is focused on shifting from reactive to proactive care, empowering individuals post-hospital stay to confidently resume everyday activities.  The council's team utilised Microsoft Copilot Studio to develop in-house AI tools, including predictive analytics. AI-powered predictive analytics were used to match individuals with optimal TEC solutions and develop cost models for care planning. This integration aims to optimise TEC's impact and embed it within the council's next-generation digital strategy.

column2: Swindon Borough Council: Simply Readable, an AI easy-read solution
column4: Swindon Borough Council’s Simply Readable project is an innovative AI-powered solution designed to create accessible Easy-Read documents for residents with learning disabilities. By using artificial intelligence, the council dramatically reduced the time and cost of producing these documents—from weeks and hundreds of pounds to just minutes and pennies per page. Developed in collaboration with residents, the tool ensures clear language, larger fonts, and helpful visuals, making vital information easier to understand. It also supports translation into 75 languages, promoting broader inclusivity across the community.

column2: Tunbridge Wells Borough Council: The Amelia Scott, Collecting Tomorrow
column4: Tunbridge Wells Borough Council’s “Collecting Tomorrow” project is a cutting-edge initiative at The Amelia Scott cultural hub, where artificial intelligence is revolutionising artifact preservation and visitor engagement. By integrating generative AI into 3D scanning workflows, the team has dramatically improved the speed and accuracy of creating digital twins of historical objects. These scans are used in immersive exhibitions, tactile displays, and even virtual reality experiences. The project has also empowered the local community through training, boosted career opportunities, and opened doors for commercial services—making it a model of digital innovation in the cultural sector.

column2: Warwickshire County Council: “Access to Food” – exploring LLMs for citizen needs
column4: Warwickshire County Council’s “Access to Food” project explores the use of open-source large language models (LLMs) to better understand how citizens—especially in deprived areas—access healthy and affordable food options. By deploying the Llama 2 model in-house, the council maintained full data sovereignty while experimenting with AI-driven classification of local businesses. Although traditional data science methods outperformed the LLM in accuracy, the project revealed promising potential for automating manual tasks and saving staff time. It also highlighted key challenges around hardware, security, and governance, offering valuable insights for responsible AI adoption in local government.

column2: Wigan Council: QuickAction in adult social care
column4: Wigan Council’s QuickAction initiative is a transformative AI-powered project developed in partnership with Agilisys Transform to enhance adult social care services. The suite of tools automates key administrative tasks—such as needs assessments, meeting minutes, case audits, and survey analysis—freeing up staff time for direct resident support. By involving social workers in the design process, the tools are tailored to real-world needs and have already saved over 100 staff days. The project also includes an AI chatbot and extends benefits to housing services, while robust governance ensures ethical and secure AI use.

column2: SignPort: Transforming access to BSL interpreters through technology
column4: In 2022, the Scottish Government’s Directorate for Equality, Inclusion & Human Rights challenged organisations to explore how technology could improve the way British Sign Language (BSL) users connect with interpreters. Booking interpreters is essential for BSL users to fully participate in work, social, and public life, yet existing systems are fragmented and inefficient—leaving many users unable to access interpreters even when they are available. To address this, Deaf Action partnered with Burrotech to create SignPort, a mobile app that quickly matches BSL users with the right interpreter at the right time. More than just efficiency, the solution prioritises choice, control, and equity for Deaf BSL users. The team is now expanding the platform with additional features to make it attractive to interpreters and scalable for agencies such as the NHS, including a fully integrated web version.
column8: Scottish Government’s Directorate for Equality, Inclusion & Human Rights

column2: DeepMiner: Transforming business support through data integration
column4: In 2022, the Scottish Government’s Directorate for COVID Business Resilience and Support set a challenge: how could data about businesses be used to improve the design, delivery, and evaluation of business support services across the public sector? Business data in Scotland was found to be fragmented across multiple organisations, limiting efficiency and effective service delivery. DeepMiner responded with the Data Spine, a single point of truth for all businesses in Scotland. By integrating and structuring economic data from diverse sources, the platform enables policymakers to access comprehensive insights, anticipate economic shocks, and design targeted policy responses. Beyond government, the solution offers significant economic and societal value by breaking down data silos and enabling smarter decision-making across organisations. Founded in 2017 through CivTech, DeepMiner pivoted in 2020 to focus on advanced AI-driven data technologies. Today, its team of five continues to innovate, making complex business data more accessible, usable, and impactful for Scotland’s economy.
column8: Scottish Government’s Directorate for Equality, Inclusion & Human Rights
column11: Executive and legislative organs, financial and fiscal affairs, external affairs.

column2: ConsultAI: Bath and North East Somerset Council parking charge proposals
column4: In 2024, Bath and North East Somerset Council ran a consultation on proposed parking charge changes, receiving over 4,300 responses and more than 18,000 free-text comments. Analysing such a large volume of qualitative feedback would normally take weeks of manual effort, slowing down decision-making and limiting transparency. To address this, the Council partnered with The Future Fox, who deployed their AI-powered platform ConsultAI. Within just three days, the system processed all responses and produced editable reports in Excel and Word formats, complete with thematic summaries, analysis of longer comments, and an overarching synthesis of the issues raised. This saved the council around six weeks of work while also improving the clarity, consistency, and accountability of reporting, enabling faster and more informed decisions.
column8: Bath and North East Somerset Council
column11: Executive and legislative organs, financial and fiscal affairs, external affairs.

column2: PlaceBuilder - Unlocking community-driven development
column4: Hanwood Park is set to become one of the UK’s most ambitious new housing developments, comprising 5,500 homes alongside schools, shops, employment hubs, and recreational areas. As the master developer sought planning permission, they recognized the importance of maintaining community involvement throughout the process. To support this, Hanwood Park LLP partnered with The Future Fox and deployed the PlaceBuilder digital engagement platform. The intuitive, mobile-first tool allowed them to rapidly design and launch a branded consultation—in under seven days—with polls on crucial themes like sustainability, wellbeing, and the masterplan. Cost-effective social media promotion ensured diverse community participation, while built-in analytics offered real-time insights and saved weeks of manual analysis. The result was a highly engaging consultation capturing a broad range of voices, enabling the developer to act on meaningful, representative feedback and stay responsive to local needs with clarity and confidence.

column2: Strategic spatial planning: inclusive engagement and AI in strategic spatial planning
column4: In 2020 and 2021, the Ministry of Housing, Communities & Local Government (MHCLG) commissioned The Future Fox to lead a comprehensive digital consultation across the Oxford-Cambridge Arc—a region encompassing five counties and 3.3 million residents. The initiative aimed to inform the development of a Spatial Framework guiding the area’s long-term growth and sustainability. Utilising The Future Fox’s PlaceBuilder platform, the consultation employed a two-phase approach: engaging younger audiences through visually-driven polls and providing a formal consultation site for broader participation. Targeted social media campaigns ensured high engagement, while AI analysis processed over 66,000 responses, creating spatial heatmaps to visualise community sentiment on key themes such as environment, economy, connectivity, and place-making. Notably, the consultation achieved a 28% conversion rate, significantly outperforming traditional survey methods, and successfully engaged underrepresented groups, including individuals under 35 and ethnic minorities. This project exemplifies how digital engagement and AI can facilitate inclusive and data-rich strategic planning.
column8: Ministry of Housing, Communities & Local Government (MHCLG)

column2: Simplyhealth supports their digital transformation with PolyAI
column4: Simplyhealth, a leading UK health insurer, partnered with PolyAI to develop a customer-led voice assistant aimed at modernising their customer service operations. The voice assistant efficiently handles common FAQs and identifies vulnerable callers, directing them to appropriate departments for personalised support. This AI-driven solution has successfully resolved 25% of calls, enhancing customer experience and optimising resource allocation.

column2: AI traffic system in Pittsburgh
column4: In Pittsburgh, traffic congestion caused by the city’s unique road network of hills, bridges, and irregular layouts led to significant delays for drivers. To address this, researchers at Carnegie Mellon University developed SURTRAC, an AI-based adaptive traffic signal system. Unlike traditional pre-programmed lights, SURTRAC uses sensors and cameras to detect traffic in real time, predicting flows and adjusting signal timings dynamically. The system also enables intersections to communicate with one another, creating a coordinated but decentralised network. First deployed in 2012 across nine intersections in East Liberty, the system quickly proved effective, reducing travel times by about 25%, idling by over 40%, braking by 30%, and emissions by roughly 21%. Its success led to expansion across dozens of intersections, with federal support for further deployment. Beyond vehicle efficiency, adjustments were made to improve pedestrian wait times, and experiments are underway to integrate accessibility features through mobile apps. The project eventually spun off into a company, Rapid Flow Technologies, which has begun deploying SURTRAC in other U.S. and Canadian cities. The Pittsburgh case demonstrates how AI-driven traffic management can significantly improve mobility, reduce environmental impact, and serve as a scalable model for smarter urban infrastructure.

column2: Chatbot for Mental Health and Wellbeing Support
column4: Since 2017, facing rising suicide rates in North Lanarkshire, the Hello Lamp Post initiative introduced interactive signage at three strategic public locations and online as part of the council’s “Let’s Talk” campaign; by 2021 it provided the community—including both adults and under-18s—with constant, easy access to tailored mental health advice and resources, and its early impact was notable: within 24 hours of launch in Airdrie, one person self-identified as at-risk, roughly a third of users were directed to critical mental health support and guidance, and 23 % were promptly pointed to emergency services—results deemed significant in light of the region’s upward trend in suicides.

column2: Chatbot for Collecting Patient Feedback
column4: At the Royal United Hospital (RUH) in Bath, an NHS acute-care facility, Hello Lamp Post deployed its interactive chat platform—rooted in the Friends and Family Test (FFT)—within Urgent Care and Orthopaedic Outpatient wards to ease administrative burdens, automate survey delivery, and reduce nurses’ workload; as a result, feedback completion rates skyrocketed from a mere 2 % to 70 %, each department saved around 300 staff-hours annually (equating to £11 per hour in staffing cost savings), and the hospital gathered detailed insights—58 % of orthopaedic patients rated their care as good, while 5 % highlighted areas for improvement—demonstrating how an engaging digital assistant can transform patient engagement and operational efficiency

column2: ExploreStation - Virtual Reality (VR) in Designing the Stations of the Future
column4: ExploreStation is a national engagement project that uses virtual reality to involve the public in shaping the future design of small and medium-sized railway stations in the UK. Through immersive VR experiences, people can walk through proposed station layouts, explore platforms and waiting areas, and experience the space as real users. This approach makes complex design decisions accessible to everyone and helps gather meaningful feedback on accessibility, comfort, safety, and community integration. The insights collected are used to support architects and planners in creating stations that are functional, inclusive, and genuinely shaped by the needs of the communities they serve.

column2: Virtual Reality (VR) in the Nottinghamshire Hospice
column4: The Nottinghamshire Hospice project showcases how Digital Urban supported the hospice in exploring the use of immersive and digital tools to enhance care environments and community engagement. Nottinghamshire Hospice provides free palliative and end-of-life care across the region, and the collaboration focused on using digital visualisation to help communicate the hospice’s spaces, services, and impact in a more accessible and meaningful way. Through this work, Digital Urban helped the organisation strengthen its connection with patients, families, supporters and the wider community.

column2: Virtual Reality (VR) in the Paxton’s Conservatory at Chatsworth House
column4: The Chatsworth project brings the long-lost Great Conservatory back to life through detailed 3D modelling and immersive virtual reality. Originally designed by Joseph Paxton in the early 19th century, the Great Conservatory was once the largest glasshouse in the world, filled with exotic plants, engineered heating systems and dramatic architectural forms. Although the structure was demolished in 1920, Digital Urban used historic drawings, archival material and landscape data to rebuild it digitally with remarkable accuracy. The VR experience allows visitors to step inside the conservatory as it once stood, exploring its soaring iron framework, glass vaults and lush interior planting. By merging historical research with advanced visualisation techniques, the project not only recreates a lost architectural masterpiece but also helps modern audiences understand its scale, engineering innovation and cultural significance. It serves as a vivid example of how immersive technologies can revive heritage spaces, opening up new ways to experience and interpret the past.

column2: Virtual Reality (VR) in Seacombe
column4: The Seacombe project is a regeneration‑oriented initiative that uses immersive 3D modelling and virtual‑reality technology to visualise a possible future for Seacombe (and nearby New Brighton). Through this VR experience, residents and stakeholders are invited to “walk through” conceptual redesigns of waterfront, urban and public‑realm spaces — traveling (virtually) along promenades or waterways and seeing how redevelopment proposals might transform the area. The project aims to engage the local community, gather their feedback, and help shape longterm planning and regeneration proposals in a participatory way.

column2: Virtual Reality (VR) in New Brighton
column4: Digital Urban worked with the Wirral Borough Council and stakeholders to create a 3‑D model and immersive virtual reality experience for the New Brighton area (together with Seacombe), showing how the town’s waterfront, promenade and public spaces could be reimagined. The VR experience allowed participants to take a virtual tour along the New Brighton promenade (on a simulated open‑top bus) and to visualise possible future redevelopment ideas — based on input from local young people and planning proposals. The purpose was to support public consultation and engagement on a regeneration masterplan, helping residents and stakeholders understand and react to proposals in a more intuitive, immersive way.

column2: Virtual Reality (VR) in Visualising the Future of Farnham High Street
column4: Digital Urban created a digital twin of Farnham town centre and then used immersive virtual‑reality experiences to let the public explore proposed regeneration plans for the high street. At pop-up events directly on the high street, participants could use VR (or joystick‑controlled versions) to “fly” — even with a jet‑pack simulation — or walk / cycle through a visualised version of the future Farnham. The goal was to open up the consultation in a playful, accessible way, enabling a broader and more diverse group of residents (especially younger people) to understand, visualise and provide feedback on proposed changes.

column2: AI4Gov - Trusted AI for Transparent Public Governance fostering Democratic Values
column4: The AI4Gov project is a European Union–funded initiative running from January 2023 to December 2025, focused on advancing trustworthy, transparent, and ethical AI in public governance. It develops tools and frameworks to help policymakers create evidence-based, automated policies while safeguarding human rights, democratic values, and compliance with regulations like GDPR and the EU AI Act. The project addresses bias, discrimination, and trust issues in AI and Big Data, aiming to increase citizen engagement and confidence in democratic processes by providing transparent, interpretable, and user-friendly AI solutions.
column47: Consortium (research institutions, private sector and other PAs)

column2: AI4Gov - Trusted AI for Transparent Public Governance fostering Democratic Values (Spain)
column4: Diputación Provincial de Badajoz (DPB) is a local government body coordinating municipalities to promote economic and social development across the province. As part of the AI4Gov project, the DPB pilot focuses on sustainable water cycle management, covering both drinking and wastewater. The council oversees 63 drinking water treatment plants serving 137,910 residents, along with wastewater treatment. The pilot aims to develop intelligent tools that leverage existing water management systems and data to identify inefficiencies—such as leaks, water losses, or off-hour energy use—enabling technicians to implement non-obvious, day-to-day solutions. The insights will also support policymakers in infrastructure planning, budget allocation, and optimising water services by training AI models to detect system malfunctions.

The insights will also support policymakers in infrastructure planning, budget allocation, and optimising water services by training AI models to detect system malfunctions.

column2: AI4Gov - Trusted AI for Transparent Public Governance fostering Democratic Values (Slovenia - Pilot1)
column4: The Jožef Stefan Institute (JSI), in partnership with UNESCO’s International Research Centre in Artificial Intelligence (IRCAI), focuses on advancing AI for societal benefit. Within AI4Gov, JSI is piloting three initiatives. The first pilot, Top100 Projects, builds on IRCAI’s annual showcase of 100 AI initiatives addressing the UN’s 17 Sustainable Development Goals. AI4Gov enhances the application and evaluation system to create a global platform that emphasises ethical practices and practical impact.
column47: Consortium (research institutions, private sector and other PAs)

column2: AI4Gov - Trusted AI for Transparent Public Governance fostering Democratic Values (Slovenia - Pilot2)
column4: The Jožef Stefan Institute (JSI), in partnership with UNESCO’s International Research Centre in Artificial Intelligence (IRCAI), focuses on advancing AI for societal benefit. Within AI4Gov, JSI is piloting three initiatives. The second pilot, the JSI SDG Observatory, is a web-based toolkit that gathers and visualises indicators to monitor progress toward the Sustainable Development Goals, providing policymakers and researchers with actionable insights.
column47: Consortium (research institutions, private sector and other PAs)

column2: AI4Gov - Trusted AI for Transparent Public Governance fostering Democratic Values (Slovenia - Pilot3)
column4: The Jožef Stefan Institute (JSI), in partnership with UNESCO’s International Research Centre in Artificial Intelligence (IRCAI), focuses on advancing AI for societal benefit. Within AI4Gov, JSI is piloting three initiatives. The third pilot focuses on OECD AI policy analysis, examining national AI strategies with an emphasis on bias. It produces visual summaries of how policies address bias and highlights examples of best practices, supporting more informed and ethical AI governance.
column47: Consortium (research institutions, private sector and other PAs)

column2: AI4Gov - Trusted AI for Transparent Public Governance in Transport (Greece - Pilot1)
column4: Vari-Voula-Vouliagmeni (VVV) is a municipality in eastern Attica with 50,585 residents and a significant influx of seasonal and weekend visitors drawn to its coastline, cultural sites, and events. The municipality faces the challenge of optimising policies across transportation, waste management, sanitation, healthcare, security, and public works to adapt dynamically to varying citizen flows, including spatiotemporal patterns and ad-hoc situations like severe weather. Coordinating updates across domains—for example, aligning parking management with waste collection—is crucial. Within AI4Gov, VVV proposed a use case focuing on parking ticket monitoring. The aim is to predict citizen flows and design efficient, multi-domain policies to manage people and vehicle movement effectively.
column47: Consortium (research institutions, private sector and other PAs)

column2: AI4Gov - Trusted AI for Transparent Public Governance in Waste Management (Greece - Pilot2)
column4: Vari-Voula-Vouliagmeni (VVV) is a municipality in eastern Attica with 50,585 residents and a significant influx of seasonal and weekend visitors drawn to its coastline, cultural sites, and events. The municipality faces the challenge of optimising policies across transportation, waste management, sanitation, healthcare, security, and public works to adapt dynamically to varying citizen flows, including spatiotemporal patterns and ad-hoc situations like severe weather. Coordinating updates across domains—for example, aligning parking management with waste collection—is crucial. Within AI4Gov, VVV proposed a use case focusing on waste management. The aim is to predict citizen flows and design efficient, multi-domain policies to manage people and vehicle movement effectively.
column47: Consortium (research institutions, private sector and other PAs)

column2: Machine Learning towards a greener grid
column4: The “Towards a Greener Grid” project highlights how the Turing Institute collaborated with the National Grid Electricity System Operator to develop advanced data-driven forecasting models for renewable energy, particularly solar and wind power. By applying machine-learning and statistical techniques, the team created a solar-forecasting system that improved the accuracy of day-ahead solar energy predictions by around 33%. These improved forecasts help the grid operator balance electricity supply and demand more effectively, reduce reliance on backup generation, lower costs for consumers, and support the integration of a higher share of renewable energy into the UK grid.
column47: Consortium (research institutions, private sector and other PAs)

column2: TrackIT Blockchain - The value chain for the traceability of Made in Italy
column4: TrackIT blockchain is a program offered by Agenzia ICE that enables selected Italian exporters — especially in sectors like fashion, agro‑food, cosmetics, furniture and design — to adopt blockchain-based traceability for their supply chains. The idea is to register key production and provenance data on a tamper‑proof blockchain ledger and link this information to products via a QR code or smart tag. In doing so, companies can transparently show the origin, production process and authenticity of their goods, thereby enhancing trust among international buyers and consumers, protecting the “Made in Italy” brand, and creating a clear, verifiable narrative around quality and supply‑chain integrity.
column47: Consortium (research institutions, private sector and other PAs)

column2: Block4Mat - Blockchain technology for the ceramics and construction supply chain
column4: BLOCH4MAT is a research and development project focused on applying blockchain technology to the supply chain of construction materials, particularly ceramic tiles, bricks, and composite building materials. The project aims to create a blockchain-based platform that ensures full traceability and digital certification of materials from raw material sourcing through production, processing, and final use, including end-of-life or disposal. Its objectives include increasing transparency, securing data, optimising processes for producers and stakeholders, and integrating blockchain with digital construction tools like BIM to enable smarter lifecycle management of building materials. The project runs for 24 months, from February 2024 to February 2026, and is coordinated by Centro Ceramico with the participation of research institutions and industry partners, together with Centers for Innovation of the High Technology Network of the Emilia-Romagna Region.
column47: Consortium (research institutions, private sector and other PAs)

column2: BIM-To-Digital Twin: towards predictive and intelligent management of real estate assets
column4: The project explores how combining Building Information Modeling (BIM) with Internet-of-Things (IoT) technologies can enable a predictive and intelligent management of real estate assets. Applying this approach to the University of Florence’s assets, the initiative employs Infocad.FM together with BIM tools to create reliable, up-to-date building data. By doing so, the project aims to support facility management by turning static building models into dynamic digital representations that can monitor conditions, schedule maintenance more efficiently, and anticipate needs — effectively forming a basis for a “digital twin” of the building heritage.

column2: Digital Twin of the City of Parma
column4: The Digital Twin of Parma is an innovative project, launched in 2024, aimed at creating a highly detailed three-dimensional virtual model of the entire city, including buildings, roads, green areas, infrastructure, and public services. The first phase of data collection — involving panoramic surveys and point-cloud acquisition across streets and pedestrian zones — has been successfully completed, with a second phase planned for spring 2025. The digital twin will serve as a strategic tool for urban planning and public administration. It will allow the city to simulate the effects of planning decisions, improve service management, optimise maintenance activities, and support environmental and sustainability objectives, including progress towards carbon neutrality. By providing an accurate and constantly updated representation of the urban environment, the project enables more efficient, transparent, and forward-looking governance of the city of Parma.

column2: BeTwin - Building Digital Twin for Built Heritage Performance-based Management
column4: BeTwin is a project coordinated by the University of Bologna that aims to create a digital‑twin ecosystem for built heritage and complex real‑estate portfolios, supporting data‑driven, performance‑based management of buildings. The initiative integrates multiple sources of information, including architectural and structural models through Building Information Modeling (BIM), energy performance simulations, and, where available, sensor and occupancy data. This unified digital environment allows building managers to make informed decisions about maintenance, energy use, functional requirements, and environmental impact. BeTwin focuses on large, multifunctional, or publicly used properties such as university buildings, schools, offices, and museums, where traditional facility management practices often struggle to balance operational efficiency with sustainability goals. The project is developing an open-source digital twin toolkit that uses knowledge graphs to represent building data, enables integrated performance analysis, and provides user-friendly interfaces for visualising key performance indicators, making advanced building management accessible even to non-technical users.

column2: 4D digital model for the archaeological site of Cencelle
column4: The project develops a 4D digital model of the archaeological site of Cencelle, located in Tarquinia, Viterbo, by integrating advanced technologies such as drone-based multisensor surveys, photogrammetry, thermal imaging, and laser scanning with traditional on-site documentation and historical‑architectural analysis. The digital twin captures the current state of the ruins and allows for multitemporal monitoring, enabling the detection of degradation, assessment of environmental impacts, and planning of preventive conservation or restoration interventions. The initiative supports long-term, sustainable management and preservation of Cencelle’s architectural and archaeological heritage, providing researchers, conservators, and administrators with an effective tool to monitor, analyse, and safeguard the site over time.
column47: Consortium (research institutions, private sector and other PAs)

column2: Milan Smart City Project: 2,500 km of roads surveyed by Cyclomedia mapping
column4: The city of Milan, in collaboration with Cyclomedia, has undertaken a project to map approximately 2,500 km of urban roads to create a comprehensive digital twin of the city.
