column4: In Odesa, Ukraine, authorities have launched a pilot telemedicine project called BrainScan that uses artificial intelligence to dramatically speed up the diagnosis of brain diseases and injuries — a critical factor when every minute can save a life. The system, developed under Ukraine’s national telemedicine strategy and supported by USAID, uses a neural network to automatically analyse CT scans of the brain and detect pathological changes. Within about five minutes after scanning, the AI’s results allow doctors to make faster, more accurate treatment decisions, helping reduce medical errors and giving clinicians a valuable “second opinion.” Following its initial success in Odesa, this AI diagnostic approach has also been introduced in frontline Kramatorsk in Donetsk region.

column2: AI helps diagnose tuberculosis and other lung pathologies
column4: The project uses artificial intelligence (AI) to assist in diagnosing Tuberculosis (TB) and other lung diseases by analysing chest X‑rays. Several AI‑powered systems have been installed across Ukrainian phthisiopulmonology clinics and mobile X‑ray units, enabling faster, more accurate and more sensitive detection of lung pathologies — even subtle changes that might be missed by the human eye. In 2024 alone, the AI systems analysed thousands of images with high accuracy (around 92.5%), significantly easing the burden on radiologists, accelerating diagnosis and allowing earlier initiation of treatment. The technology also supports outreach efforts, as mobile teams can scan and screen patients in remote or underserved communities, improving access to crucial diagnostics.
column8: Public Health Center of the Ministry of Health of Ukraine (PHC)

column2: CheckEye: The first AI-based platform in Europe implementing a national chronic disease screening
column4: CheckEye is the first AI-based platform in Europe to implement a national screening program for chronic diseases and their complications, first deployed in Ukraine in 2024 across six regions during wartime. It focuses on early detection of diabetic retinopathy, using artificial intelligence to enable large-scale, accurate screening and help prevent vision loss. After its national rollout in Ukraine, CheckEye has expanded internationally with implementations and pilot projects in Austria, Germany, Romania, Oman, and Malaysia.

column2: New cross-government, AI-powered data management asset
column4: The Estonian government is preparing to launch a new cross‑government, AI‑enabled data‑management platform designed to make public‑sector data from different ministries and agencies searchable and accessible to citizens through a single unified interface. The tool aims to break down silos, allowing individuals to retrieve information and access services regardless of which particular agency holds the data — health, social services, veterans affairs, etc. The first components of the system are expected to become operational this year, with further rollout and improvements to follow. The initiative reflects Estonia’s long‑standing commitment to digital governance and seeks to improve transparency, efficiency, and citizen access to government information.

column2: ChatGPT Edu into Estonia's education system
column4: The project plans to introduce ChatGPT Edu – a version of ChatGPT built for educational use – across Estonia’s secondary‑school system. Starting in 2025, students (initially 10th and 11th graders) and their teachers will be granted access to ChatGPT Edu, alongside support for custom AI tools designed for teaching and learning. Through this initiative, the aim is to embed AI into the national education system, helping students with learning and creativity, easing teachers’ workload (e.g. administrative tasks, lesson‑planning), and equipping the next generation with AI literacy and skills for the future. The project is part of Estonia’s AI Leap 2025 initiative to integrate AI capabilities into education, providing free AI tools and a framework for their use in learning, launched by the President of Estonia as a public-private sector collaboration.

column2: Machine Thinking Lab to accelerate the advancement of AI technologies
column4: The project represents a strategic partnership between the Government of Albania and the AI startup Machine Thinking Lab, founded by former OpenAI CTO Mira Murati. As part of this collaboration, Albania has committed a financial investment of approximately €8.8 million to support the startup’s efforts in developing cutting‑edge artificial intelligence systems. The initiative is designed to accelerate the advancement of AI technologies, strengthen Albania’s role in the rapidly evolving global AI landscape, and attract further expertise, innovation, and investment to the country. By backing a globally oriented AI company led by a recognised expert in the field, the project not only seeks to foster technological innovation but also aims to build local capacity, create opportunities for collaboration with international AI leaders, and position Albania as a forward‑looking player in the international AI ecosystem. Over time, the initiative is expected to contribute to both economic development and the establishment of a strong national presence in emerging AI technologies.

column2: A new supercomputer and AI factory to boost technological power
column4: The Slovenian Artificial Intelligence Factory (SLAIF) is a national initiative launched in 2025 to establish a high‑performance supercomputer alongside a dedicated AI factory. The project aims to provide cutting‑edge computing infrastructure to support research, innovation, and the development of advanced AI across industry, academia, and public services. By combining powerful computational resources with access to large datasets and collaboration between universities, research institutes, and industry partners, SLAIF will enable AI model development, data analytics, and support for startups and businesses. The initiative is designed to strengthen Slovenia’s digital sovereignty, foster scientific and technological advancement, and enhance the country’s contribution to Europe’s broader AI ecosystem.
column8: Ministry of Digital Transformation and Ministry of Higher Education, Science and Innovation

column2: Drones and AI to tackle congestion
column4: The Helsinki Drone- and AI-Based Traffic Monitoring Project uses unmanned aerial vehicles and artificial intelligence to analyse real-time mobility patterns in and around the city’s busiest transport areas, including the harbour district. The drones capture video of cars, trams, cyclists, scooters and pedestrians, and AI systems then process this footage into anonymised traffic data. The project creates a detailed digital representation of urban traffic conditions, enabling planners to simulate congestion scenarios, test mobility solutions and design safer, more efficient transport flows. Its overall aim is to reduce congestion, support sustainable mobility choices and improve the management of Helsinki’s urban transport network.

column2: Digital Twin of the Italian Soil
column4: The “Digital Twin of Italian Soil” is a national geospatial initiative led by Leonardo through its subsidiary e‑GEOS in support of the Department for Sustainable Development (DiSS) within Italy’s Ministry of the Environment and Energy Security (MASE). It forms part of the Italian National Recovery and Resilience Plan (PNRR) investment M2C4 I.1.1 and aims to create a high‑resolution digital model of Italy’s terrain using airborne LiDAR surveys and aerogravimetric data. This detailed digital representation will improve understanding of land morphology, support environmental protection, territorial planning, flood risk analysis, and infrastructure stability modelling, and serve as the foundation for future digital twins and advanced monitoring systems. The resulting databases will be interoperable with existing geospatial data and available for use by public and private stakeholders to enhance resilience and sustainability across the country.

column2: Gemello Digitale Esteso - Milan's Digital Twin
column4: Milan is developing a comprehensive digital twin of its metropolitan area under a municipal smart‑city initiative. The project, led by the Municipality of Milan with four geoinformation partners, combines high‑resolution aerial imagery, LiDAR point clouds, and terrestrial mobile mapping data to build a detailed dynamic model of the city and surrounding municipalities. This virtual representation supports urban planning, resource management and decision‑making by providing interactive geospatial datasets and software tools for analysis. The digital twin covers about 1,575 km² and includes thousands of urban objects, helping city authorities make better data‑driven choices and improve quality of life for residents and stakeholders.

column2: TOURISMO Project - Florence
column4: The Municipality of Florence is piloting new smart technologies, including thermal cameras and “sniffers”, to monitor and manage tourist flows in the historic centre as part of the TOURISMO – TOURism Innovative and Sustainable Management of flOws project funded by Interreg Euro‑MED. Thermal cameras in Piazza Duomo and sensors across key sites such as Ponte Vecchio and Piazza Pitti collect anonymised data using AI algorithms to analyse visitor movements and behaviours. This information is made available to city authorities to improve liveability, address overtourism challenges, and support sustainable tourism planning. The initiative also includes interactive tools like QR codes for real‑time visitor feedback, and aims to produce a replicable operational manual for use in other European contexts

column2: TOURISMO Project - Malta
column4: As part of the TOURISMO – Tourism Innovative and Sustainable Management of flOws programme, a pilot beach monitoring system was launched at Golden Bay to help manage overtourism and protect the area’s natural environment. The initiative, led by the Malta Regional Development and Dialogue Foundation (MRDDf) and Il‑Majjistral Nature and History Park, uses GDPR‑compliant people‑counting CCTV and deep‑learning algorithms to monitor pedestrian and vehicle traffic, providing real‑time data on visitor densities and behaviours. This information supports sustainable tourism planning and conservation of the bay’s unique dune ecosystems, with input from local stakeholders including the Mellieħa Local Council, Environmental Resources Authority, Cleansing Department, and Malta Tourism Authority.

column2: Open Rights Data Exchange - A Common European data space for copyright
column4: The Open Rights Data Exchange (ORDE) is a pilot initiative built on the European Blockchain Services Infrastructure (EBSI) that aims to create a common European data space for copyright. Its goal is to improve how rights data — authorship, licensing, royalty allocations and content metadata — are managed, shared and verified. ORDE allows creators and rights-holders to register their works (text, images, audio, video, etc.) by submitting a content fingerprint and a verified identity; these bindings are timestamped and stored immutably on the blockchain ledger. This enables online platforms, distributors and other users to retrieve accurate, interoperable and machine-readable rights and licensing information, streamlining licensing processes, reducing costs and fostering transparent monetisation of creative works.

column2: Digital Ferry Onboarding Experience
column4: The Digital Ferry Onboarding Experience aims to modernise ferry travel for university students by enabling identity verification, ticket booking and boarding via a digital wallet. Through a partnership between a ferry operator, a European university alliance and EBSI, students receive “verifiable credentials” that prove their status (e.g. student identity), which they store in a digital wallet. When booking a ferry, the system verifies their credentials, automatically applies relevant discounts, issues a digital boarding pass, and allows seamless boarding by simply presenting the pass from their wallet — eliminating paper tickets and manual checks. The project showcases how blockchain-based identity wallets, standardised credentials and decentralised verification can streamline travel procedures, enhance security, simplify cross-border mobility, and improve the overall passenger experience.

column2: Milan-Cortina 2026: Digital twin applied to construction site safety and transparency
column4: The Italian Ministry of Infrastructure and Transport (MIT) is using a digital twin platform to modernise and monitor the 98 infrastructure projects (cantieri) being built for the Milano‑Cortina 2026 Winter Olympics. This technology integrates design data, sensors and 3D models into a dynamic, interactive virtual replica of the construction sites, updated in real time to track progress and support transparency. Beyond engineering oversight, the twin offers immersive experiences that allow the public to “enter” the virtual sites and explore the works virtually, enhancing engagement and showcasing the development of the Olympic legacy. The project was highlighted in August 2025 as part of MIT’s innovation programme ahead of the Games.
column8: Italian Ministry of Infrastructure and Transport (MIT)

column2: Utrecht Digital Twin
column4: The Municipality of Utrecht is developing an interactive 3D digital twin of the city that integrates above‑ground and underground infrastructure, buildings, parks, and climate data to support data‑driven urban planning and sustainability. The platform allows city officials, researchers and residents to visualise planned changes, assess environmental impacts, and test scenarios before implementation, helping accelerate infrastructure decision‑making and guide climate adaptation measures such as heat mapping and green space planning. The initiative involves partnerships with local universities, mapping agencies, technology companies (including Unity), and community contributions of climate data. Utrecht has already used the twin to streamline planning processes, incorporate neighbourhood climate datasets and inform strategies for tree planting, cooling infrastructure, and prioritising green spaces — and the model continues to evolve with ongoing data integration.

column2: Digital Twin for Monte Carlo Station
column4: In June 2025, SNCF Gares & Connexions partnered with Akila and NVIDIA to deploy an AI‑powered digital twin platform at Monte‑Carlo train station in Monaco. The system uses real‑time simulation, AI agents and sensor data to create a dynamic 3D model of the station’s built environment. It ingests diverse inputs — including crowd movement, airflow, solar heating and IoT sensor feeds — and visualises them via NVIDIA Omniverse to support predictive analytics, energy optimisation, preventive maintenance and emergency simulations. Early results include reduced energy use, faster response times to operational issues, and lower downtime through predictive maintenance. The platform also enables improved safety, crowd control and efficiency planning across transport infrastructure

column2: AI-Powered Digital Twin of the Ocean
column4: The European Digital Twin of the Ocean (EU DTO) is an advanced AI‑powered virtual replica of the ocean, designed to simulate and explore potential “what‑if” scenarios affecting marine environments and human activity. By integrating extensive ocean data from satellites, in‑situ observations, and initiatives such as the Copernicus Marine Service and EMODnet, with numerical models and artificial intelligence, the DTO enables rapid visualisation and forecasting of ocean conditions on timescales ranging from seasonal to multi‑decade. It supports policymakers, researchers, environmental agencies, businesses, and citizens in making informed decisions, allowing simulation of scenarios such as marine pollution drift, coastal erosion mitigation, biodiversity protection, and climate impact assessment. The system operates efficiently on high‑performance GPUs, delivering forecasts in seconds rather than hours, while reducing energy consumption compared with traditional supercomputing methods. Presented at the United Nations Ocean Conference in 2025, the DTO exemplifies a major step forward in sustainable, data-driven ocean management.

column2: TWIN4DEM: Strengthening Democratic Resilience Through Digital Twins
column4: TWIN4DEM is a European research initiative funded under Horizon Europe that aims to strengthen democratic resilience across Europe by using digital twin technology and computational social science to study and address democratic backsliding. The project brings together social scientists, humanities experts and technologists to develop GDPR‑compliant tools, agent‑based models and interactive simulations of democratic systems in Czechia, France, Hungary and the Netherlands. By simulating political processes and policy scenarios, TWIN4DEM helps policymakers, civil society and researchers anticipate threats to democratic governance, promote transparency and encourage participatory engagement. The initiative runs from January 2025 to December 2027 and involves an open‑source community for wider collaboration.

column2: Dryads - AI-powered platform to manage forest resilience and mitigate climate change impacts
column4: Dryads is an AI-powered environmental monitoring platform developed by the SCAN Laboratory at the National and Kapodistrian University of Athens in cooperation with the Hellenic Ministry of Environment and Energy. Launched in 2024, it aims to strengthen forest resilience and mitigate climate change impacts, particularly wildfire risks. The platform uses AI and digital twin technologies to integrate IoT sensors, drone imagery, and environmental data into 3D geospatial models. This enables continuous forest monitoring, early wildfire detection, and fire evolution prediction, helping authorities intervene effectively. Dryads also supports vulnerability assessment and prevention strategies for forests and surrounding communities.

column2: Valencia shows how integrated geospatial systems can be used to build AI-empowered cities
column4: Valencia used geospatial technologies, drones and AI‑driven spatial analysis to support emergency response during and after the October 2024 DANA floods, one of the most severe flood events in recent local history. High‑resolution drone imagery and sensor data were rapidly analysed to locate debris, abandoned vehicles and areas heavily affected by flooding, which helped emergency teams prioritise their response and decision‑making more efficiently. The integration of these technologies also enabled the creation of detailed 3D models to assess infrastructure damage and supported hydrological simulations to better understand flood dynamics. In the wake of the disaster, ongoing initiatives in the region are exploring how AI, digital twins and predictive modelling can improve early warning systems, refine flood‑risk maps and support climate adaptation efforts for future extreme weather events.

column2: Nottingham shows how integrated geospatial systems can be used to build AI-empowered cities
column4: Nottingham City Council has developed a city‑wide digital twin, combining detailed 3D geospatial data, GIS technology and planning information to transform how urban redevelopment and decision‑making are conducted. The digital model visualises existing conditions and proposed changes across thousands of buildings and infrastructure elements, enabling planners to assess the impacts of new developments, improve transparency in public consultations, and streamline planning approvals. It has been used to support major regeneration projects — including transport hubs, housing and public spaces — attract investment and enhance strategic planning. Through this digital twin, Nottingham accelerates planning processes, supports interactive discussions with developers, and increases engagement by showing real‑world simulations of future scenarios.

column2: Digital Twin for the Reconstruction of Ukraine
column4: The European Union’s Digital Twin for the Reconstruction of Ukraine is an AI‑enabled initiative led by the European AI Office to support Ukraine’s rebuilding efforts. It uses satellite imagery and AI models to create detailed 3D virtual replicas of cities that help planners assess damage, improve urban planning, track reconstruction projects, and estimate costs with greater precision. The platform also enables citizen participation by allowing people to view and comment on reconstruction proposals in an immersive virtual environment, enhancing transparency and accountability in the recovery process.

column2: CretanPass
column4: CretanPass is a blockchain‑based solution launched in 2023, designed to provide tamper‑proof digital identities for tourists visiting Crete. Under CretanPass, travellers receive a unified digital identity and wallet that simplifies hotel check‑ins and activity bookings across the island. Since its introduction, it has significantly reduced average check-in times by 60%, while hotels have reported 40% fewer administrative errors and substantially improved guest satisfaction.

column2: Random Red Ltd. - Measurements and Digital Calibration Certificates
column4: Random Red Ltd participates in the EBSI Early Adopters Programme with a focus on enhancing metrology and calibration processes across Europe. Their project aims to use blockchain technology to improve the reliability, transparency and cross-border recognition of scientific measurement and calibration credentials. In practice, this means issuing and verifying digital calibration certificates and laboratory credentials via EBSI, facilitating trusted, tamper-proof and interoperable accreditation and conformity-assessment processes across different countries.

column2: e-Origin - Building the foundation of a cross-border e-commerce verification services for customs declarations using EBSI
column4: e-Origin is a pilot project built on the European Blockchain Services Infrastructure (EBSI) aimed at modernising and securing cross-border e-commerce customs procedures. It targets customs brokers, online sellers, marketplaces and customs authorities across the EU, using blockchain-based verifiable credentials and digital wallets to issue, exchange and verify shipment and commercial transaction data. By doing so, e-Origin seeks to prevent fraud (e.g. undervaluation of imported goods or misuse of IOSS numbers), speed up customs clearance, reduce delays and penalties, and streamline the flow of parcels entering the EU. The pilot brings together public actors (VAT and customs administrations) and private e-commerce stakeholders to build a trustworthy, auditable, interoperable e-commerce customs ecosystem.

column2: TRACE4EU - Building the foundation of traceable seafood supply-chain infrastructure
column4: TRACE4EU Seafood Tracing is a pilot application under the broader European Blockchain Services Infrastructure (EBSI) framework that aims to build a next-generation, blockchain-based supply-chain infrastructure for seafood across Europe. The project uses EBSI’s distributed ledger and verifiable credentials (VCs), combined with per-product identifiers (e.g., QR codes), to register, track and verify each step in the life-cycle of seafood — from catch (e.g., live king crab) through processing, packaging, customs, transport, and final sale. This enables authorities, supply-chain actors, and consumers to authenticate catch certificates, trace provenance, check compliance with quotas and regulations, and guard against illegal fishing or mislabelling. By creating a pan-European, tamper-proof “digital product passport” for seafood — with a full audit trail of origin, handling, certification and logistics — the project aims to boost transparency, food safety, regulatory oversight, and consumer trust in seafood supply chains.

column2: OnePass: SME Financing
column4: OnePass SME Financing is a pilot initiative under EBSI that aims to facilitate and streamline access to finance for small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) across Europe. Using blockchain-based verifiable credentials and self-sovereign identity (SSI) technology, the project enables SMEs to store, manage, and share trusted digital credentials with public authorities, investors, and private funding bodies. The platform seeks to cut friction in cross-border investments, reduce due-diligence burden, lower transaction costs and enhance trust between investors and SMEs by providing a reliable, tamper-proof “digital wallet” of credentials — thus accelerating funding processes and supporting the growth of innovative enterprises across EU markets.
column11: Executive and legislative organs, financial and fiscal affairs, external affairs.

column2: LUMI AI Supercomputer and High-Performance Computing (HPC)
column4: The LUMI AI Factory is one of Europe’s first AI factories, launched in 2025 to accelerate AI innovation across Finland and Europe. Hosted by CSC – IT Center for Science in Kajaani, it integrates world-class computing power, high-quality datasets, and expert support into a single platform. The factory enables researchers, start-ups, SMEs, and public institutions to develop, test, and scale AI solutions responsibly, with a focus on sectors such as manufacturing, healthcare, language technologies, digital twins, and climate research. LUMI, the underlying supercomputing infrastructure, offers AI-optimised computing resources, tailored training, and “Datasets as a Service,” including Finnish and Swedish speech, pathology imaging, and European web content. AI Finland serves as the ecosystem partner, connecting companies with expertise, supporting product development, experimentation, and international collaboration. The initiative strengthens Finland’s and Europe’s leadership in AI and high-performance computing while providing industry and research stakeholders with access to advanced AI capabilities that traditional IT systems cannot support.
column47: Consortium (research institutions, private sector and other PAs)

column2: L-AIF - Luxembourg AI Factory
column4: The project Luxembourg AI Factory (L-AIF) seeks to build a secure, trusted infrastructure for artificial-intelligence development centred in Luxembourg. Its aim is to leverage the supercomputing power of MeluXina-AI to offer end-to-end AI services — from model training and fine-tuning to access to high-quality datasets, compliance and support — enabling companies, start-ups and public institutions to adopt AI solutions under strong data governance. The initiative is intended to boost Luxembourg’s digital transformation and strengthen Europe’s competitive edge in AI by offering specialised support to sectors such as finance, space, cybersecurity and the green economy.
column47: Consortium (research institutions, private sector and other PAs)

column2: HammerHAI - New AI-optimised Supercomputer
column4: HammerHAI is a European initiative aimed at accelerating the adoption of artificial intelligence in industry and scientific research. Coordinated by the High-Performance Computing Centre Stuttgart, in partnership with several leading German institutions, it provides secure, scalable, and accessible AI resources for businesses, research institutions, and start-ups. The platform integrates high-performance computing with AI to facilitate large-scale training and inference of AI models, while ensuring compliance with GDPR and the European AI Act. HammerHAI offers technical consulting, tailored training, pre-configured AI solutions, and collaborative development tools to support AI adoption across sectors such as manufacturing, engineering, automotive, small and medium-sized enterprises, and academia. Its overarching objective is to strengthen Europe’s AI capabilities by lowering barriers to adoption, fostering collaboration, and enabling advanced industrial and scientific applications. The project is co-funded by the European High-Performance Computing Joint Undertaking, the European Commission, and several German state ministries. The AI involved is primarily applied machine learning, optimised with high-performance computing to accelerate workflows and deliver practical solutions in industrial and research contexts.
column47: Consortium (research institutions, private sector and other PAs)

column2: BRAIN++ New AI-optimised Supercomputer
column4: Bulgaria is establishing its own AI factory, a €90 million project led by INSAIT in collaboration with Sofia Tech Park. The initiative, called BRAIN++, will create a modern GPU-based AI data centre in Sofia, supporting large-scale AI research and applications. BRAIN++ combines a next-generation supercomputer, Discoverer++, designed for advanced AI workloads, with a comprehensive AI hub that allows government, educational institutions, and private companies to adopt and innovate responsibly with AI. The project aims to position Bulgaria as a regional leader in AI innovation, fostering talent development, supporting startups and SMEs, and advancing applications in areas such as Bulgarian language large language models (LLMs), robotics AI, space observation AI, manufacturing, trustworthy AI compliance, and federated AI data lakes. BRAIN++ also includes collaborative workspaces, both physical and virtual (BulgAI Sandbox), to encourage responsible AI entrepreneurship. By integrating with other European High-Performance Computing AI factories, the initiative contributes to a unified European AI ecosystem while promoting ethical and responsible AI practices.
column47: Consortium (research institutions, private sector and other PAs)

column2: MIMER New AI-optimised Supercomputer
column4: Mimer is Sweden’s AI Innovation Factory, designed to accelerate AI adoption for industry and research. It provides access to high-performance GPU infrastructure and a team of AI specialists to support companies, startups, and researchers in developing, deploying, and scaling AI solutions. The platform offers technical guidance, optimisation support, training programmes, and resources for building responsible and trustworthy AI in line with European ethical and regulatory standards. By combining advanced computing resources with expert support, Mimer enables organisations to focus on innovation rather than infrastructure, fostering AI development in both scientific and industrial contexts. The initiative also facilitates access to European high-performance computing resources, allowing fast experimentation, research-driven AI projects, and industry-scale workloads.
column47: Consortium (research institutions, private sector and other PAs)

column2: SLAIF New AI-optimised Supercomputer
column4: The Slovenian AI Factory (SLAIF) is a national initiative designed to build Slovenia’s AI ecosystem and enhance its AI capabilities across industry, research, and public institutions. Launched in 2025 as part of the EuroHPC Joint Undertaking, SLAIF integrates a state-of-the-art AI-optimized supercomputer hosted by IZUM, in collaboration with the Jožef Stefan Institute, Arnes, and several Slovenian universities. The facility will deliver extremely high computational power for AI applications and HPC tasks while being powered by renewable hydroelectric energy. SLAIF provides a comprehensive ecosystem that includes access to HPC infrastructure, AI expertise, pre-trained models, tailored cloud-based services, and sector-specific guidance for industry and research. It also focuses on developing AI talent through specialised training programmes, internships, and workshops. SLAIF supports applications in smart energy systems, agriculture, healthcare, biotechnology, pharmaceuticals, language technologies, and scientific research, promoting sustainable digital transformation and Slovenia’s integration into the broader European AI ecosystem.
column8: IZUM – the Institute of Information Science
column47: Consortium (research institutions, private sector and other PAs)

column2: PIAST AIF New AI-optimised Supercomputer
column4: The PIAST-AI Factory is Poland’s first national AI factory, hosted at the Poznań Supercomputing and Networking Center (PCSS). Launched in 2025 as part of the European Commission’s AI Factories programme, it aims to position Poland as a leader in AI technologies across fields such as IT, cybersecurity, space technologies, robotics, and sustainable development. The project is implemented in cooperation with Polish universities including Poznań University of Technology, Adam Mickiewicz University, and Nicolaus Copernicus University, alongside regional innovation clusters. PIAST-AI will deploy a high-performance AI server equipped with over 1000 graphics cards and integrated with Poland’s quantum and computing ecosystem. Scheduled to launch comprehensive AI services in 2026, the initiative focuses on fostering innovation, supporting industry and research, and promoting ethical, sustainable AI development while enhancing Poland’s digital competitiveness.
column47: Consortium (research institutions, private sector and other PAs)

column2: AI2F AI-ready Supercomputer
column4: The AI Factory France (AI2F) is part of the new network of European AI factories launched in March 2025, building on France’s decentralised AI ecosystem of companies, start-ups, research labs, data centres, universities, venture capitalists, incubators, and AI associations. Leveraging state-of-the-art supercomputing infrastructure and support services, AI2F will promote AI adoption in research, innovation, and public services across France and Europe. Led by Genci in collaboration with partners such as Amiad, CEA, CINES, CNRS, France Universités, Inria, The French Tech, Station F, and HubFranceIA, AI2F is based on Alice Recoque, the second EuroHPC Exascale supercomputer, scheduled for deployment in 2026. In the meantime, it will provide access to existing French national infrastructures: Jean Zay at IDRIS, Adastra at CINES, and Joliot-Curie at TGCC. Open to all sectors, AI2F supports collaboration to advance AI adoption in areas including defence, energy, aerospace, edtech, agriculture, finance, humanities, robotics, health, earth sciences, materials science, and mobility. As part of the European AI factory network, it will cooperate with other AI factories and engage with stakeholders across Europe, including ALT_EDIC, BDVA, EOSC, Gaia-X, EDIHs, and TEFs.
column47: Consortium (research institutions, private sector and other PAs)

column2: Pharos AI Factory
column4: The Pharos AI Factory is Greece’s national AI initiative, launched on 1 April 2025 as part of the EuroHPC AI Factories programme. It aims to create a national ecosystem that promotes AI innovation across academia, industry, and the public sector, focusing on health, Greek language and culture, and sustainable development. The project will leverage the DAEDALUS supercomputer, optimised for AI, which will be fully operational in 2026 with 89 Pflops of computing power and high-speed connectivity, enabling large-scale AI applications. The Pharos consortium is led by GRNET (National Infrastructures for Research and Technology) under the Ministry of Digital Governance, in partnership with the National Centre for Scientific Research “Demokritos”, the National Technical University of Athens, the Athena Research Center, Growthfund, and affiliated institutions including CERTH, Aristotle University of Thessaloniki, NKUA, FORTH, and the University of Piraeus. Pharos AI Factory aims to support research, industrial innovation, and new business models, offering access to HPC resources and AI tools for startups, SMEs, public institutions, and researchers. By strengthening Greece’s technological autonomy and fostering responsible AI, Pharos also contributes to job creation, brain regain, and Europe-wide AI collaboration within the continental AI factory network. The project has a total budget of €30 million, jointly funded by EuroHPC JU and Greek national resources.
column8: GRNET (National Infrastructures for Research and Technology)
column47: Consortium (research institutions, private sector and other PAs)

column2: AI:AT New AI-optimised Supercomputer
column4: The AI Factory Austria (AI:AT) is a national initiative launched on 12 March 2025 to strengthen Austria’s AI ecosystem as part of the European High-Performance Computing (EuroHPC) AI Factories programme. The project, led by Advanced Computing Austria (ACA) and the AIT Austrian Institute of Technology, brings together academic and industrial partners to establish a state-of-the-art AI-optimised supercomputer and an AI Factory Hub. The infrastructure supports research, industry, and public institutions, providing computing resources, expert support, training, and innovation services. Building on the existing Vienna Scientific Cluster (VSC), AI:AT enables large-scale AI model training and deployment for applications in biotechnology, sustainability, energy, and production efficiency. The AI Factory Hub also functions as a community centre and one-stop-shop for SMEs and start-ups, fostering collaboration and responsible, ethical AI development. The initiative integrates Austria into the wider European AI ecosystem, cooperating with other AI factories and research centres to advance innovation, competitiveness, and cross-sector AI adoption.
column47: Consortium (research institutions, private sector and other PAs)

column2: KarolAIna - petascale EuroHPC supercomputer in Czechia
column4: The Czech AI Factory (CZAI) is the Czech Republic’s national AI factory, selected in October 2025 under the European High‑Performance Computing Joint Undertaking (EuroHPC) AI Factories programme. Led by VSB – Technical University of Ostrava in collaboration with partners including the Czech Technical University in Prague, Charles University, the International Neurodegenerative Disorders Research Center, the Institute of Organic Chemistry and Biochemistry of the Czech Academy of Sciences, and Brno University of Technology, the project will establish a comprehensive AI infrastructure to support industry, research, start‑ups, public institutions and SMEs. At its core will be KarolAIna, a new supercomputer optimised for AI workloads, built on about 340 advanced AI chips and delivering high‑performance computing power for training, deploying and experimenting with AI models. CZAI will offer access to AI‑ready HPC resources, expert support, training and services across sectors such as manufacturing, energy, healthcare, public administration, cybersecurity and mobility, while also establishing AI campuses in Prague and Brno to nurture talent. The initiative strengthens the Czech AI ecosystem and deepens its integration into the wider European AI Factory network, contributing to technological innovation and economic growth.
column47: Consortium (research institutions, private sector and other PAs)

column2: Shelfex AI - Predictive AI transforming shelf life management
column4: Belgian food safety technology company Handary has implemented a predictive, AI-driven approach to transform how food shelf life is managed. By combining biotechnology, analytical science and artificial intelligence, the company provides manufacturers with enhanced visibility and control over product integrity throughout the food supply chain. At the core of the solution is Shelfex AI, a platform that authenticates ingredients, predicts spoilage before it occurs and supports compliance with date-marking requirements. The system integrates laboratory data, production parameters and distribution conditions to move shelf-life assessment from empirical estimation to predictive, data-driven decision-making. Handary’s solution is complemented by Microfier smart labels, which display real-time microbial signals directly on packaging, and Hékôn intelligent micro-perforated membranes, which regulate gas balance while monitoring storage conditions during distribution. Together, these technologies create a unified, traceable and transparent framework that strengthens food safety management and builds trust among manufacturers, regulators and consumers.

column2: Blockchain for trade
column4: Lithuanian researchers have developed a blockchain-based tracking system designed to prevent illegal timber from Russia and Belarus entering European supply chains. The system assigns each log a unique identifier and records every step — from permits to transport and sale — on the blockchain, making the supply chain tamper-resistant, transparent and verifiable. In simulation tests, it processed up to 550 transactions per second, and trades from participants with higher sustainability scores succeeded at a rate of up to 95%. The researchers argue that by combining blockchain immutability with smart-contract enforcement, such as blocking harvesting without permits, the system could close one of the EU’s largest loopholes: the roughly €1.5 billion flow of sanctioned Russian timber entering Europe since sanctions began in 2022.

column2: Blockchain for Strategy Portfolio Rebalancing
column4: 31Third provides advanced trading infrastructure for institutional on-chain asset managers and on-chain index funds. The platform leverages blockchain technology to enable transparent, automated, and efficient management of digital asset portfolios, including seamless basket trading and portfolio rebalancing. By combining on-chain automation with regulatory compliance and smart order routing, 31Third bridges traditional finance with decentralised finance (DeFi) for institutional investors. 31Third enables institutional asset managers to implement strategy-based portfolio rebalancing efficiently and cost-effectively. Asset managers can define target allocations for their portfolios, and the platform automatically adjusts holdings in a single transaction to match these targets. This approach reduces operational complexity, minimises manual intervention, and ensures that portfolios remain aligned with the investment strategy in real time. By automating the rebalancing process, 31Third enhances accuracy, reduces transaction costs, and allows managers to focus on strategic decision-making rather than administrative execution.
column11: Executive and legislative organs, financial and fiscal affairs, external affairs.

column2: Blockchain for Issuance- and Redemption-Triggered Rebalancing
column4: For on-chain ETFs (exchange-traded funds), 31Third provides automated rebalancing triggered by the issuance or redemption of fund shares. This ensures that the underlying assets of an ETF consistently reflect the fund’s intended composition without requiring manual adjustments. By integrating smart contracts and real-time market data, the platform guarantees that fund allocations remain accurate during subscription and redemption events. This functionality increases operational efficiency, maintains transparency for investors, and supports the seamless growth and management of on-chain ETFs in a fully automated and compliant manner.
column11: Executive and legislative organs, financial and fiscal affairs, external affairs.

column2: Blocksquare – Notarised Real Estate Tokenisation Framework
column4: Blocksquare has developed a notarised tokenisation framework for real estate assets, using blockchain technology to enable fractional investment, broader market access, and improved liquidity while remaining fully compliant with EU regulations. Tokens are backed by notarised loan agreements and directly registered mortgages in euros, providing investors with strong legal protection and transparency. The platform features a decentralised marketplace for secure trading of tokenised real estate, allowing businesses to launch their own branded marketplaces. Successfully piloted in Slovenia in 2023, the framework has been validated across multiple EU jurisdictions. Blocksquare also plans to extend the framework to tokenise alternative assets, such as company shares.
column11: Executive and legislative organs, financial and fiscal affairs, external affairs.

column2: CHARONIUM GmbH - Legacy - Digital Inheritance Platform
column4: CHARONIUM® Legacy is a specialised platform for the secure discovery, verification, and transfer of digital assets, including cryptocurrencies, NFTs, and tokenised real estate. Designed for notaries, legal professionals, and financial institutions, it combines blockchain transparency with regulatory compliance to automate inheritance transfers, ensure tamper-proof verification, and maintain European legal and financial standards. The platform provides a secure on-chain registry of digital estates, smart contract-based asset allocation, and cross-border compatibility, reducing legal costs, minimising disputes, and streamlining probate processes. CHARONIUM® Legacy sets a new standard for efficient, legally compliant digital estate management across the EU.
column11: Executive and legislative organs, financial and fiscal affairs, external affairs.

column2: Blockchain-Powered Digital Product Passport (DPP) for Battery Lifecycle Management
column4: Traced Systems, Instagrid, Batteriretur, and Cling Systems have developed a blockchain-powered Digital Product Passport (DPP) to transform end-of-life battery management. The pilot tracked batteries after production through collection, recycling, and secondary markets, enhancing transparency, traceability, and compliance across the battery value chain. Manufacturers gain insights into battery usage and sustainability, while collectors access detailed chemistry and lifecycle data to optimise recycling. The system also verifies battery health and provenance, supporting circularity and secondary markets. The project demonstrates that blockchain-enabled DPPs can drive efficiency, sustainability, and regulatory alignment, paving the way for a circular economy in battery management.

column2: Distributed ledger technology (DLT) to create a transparent, community-driven financial ecosystem
column4: Multi leverages distributed ledger technology to create a fully decentralised, transparent financial ecosystem where all decisions, including protocol updates, are made collectively through on-chain proposal voting. The system features a governance token for a decentralised autonomous organisation (DAO) and a fully collateralised basket token backed by a dynamic reserve of crypto assets. Asset allocations are proposed by participants and combined through a meritocratic, game-theoretic process, ensuring expertise-driven, market-oriented decision-making. The platform also functions as a decentralised exchange (DEX), issuing new tokens and dynamically adjusting supply to maintain market balance. With its decentralised frontend and backend, Multi offers security, resilience, transparency, and long-term adaptability, supporting efficient, community-governed finance.
column11: General economic, commercial and labour affair

column2: DOWSERS - blockchain for smart contracts
column4: Dowsers applies decades of formal verification expertise to the blockchain space, providing trust in smart contracts through scalable, accessible verification technology. Smart contract bugs and vulnerabilities pose significant risks to developers, investors, and financial institutions. Dowsers offers a suite of software tools and algorithms that automatically verify and formally prove the safety and correctness of smart contracts, either before or after deployment. Using rigorous, mathematical analysis, the platform examines code across all possible states and conditions, identifying common vulnerabilities, logic inconsistencies, and security risks. It generates instant, comprehensive reports to guide developers in securing contracts and inform investors of potential financial risks. Dowsers supports multiple blockchains and languages, ensuring reliable and precise smart contract verification for the entire blockchain ecosystem.
column11: General economic, commercial and labour affair

column2: Decentralised Coordination of Distributed Energy Resources
column4: Enoda ENSEMBLE™ is a DLT platform that helps balance increasingly volatile electricity demand and renewable generation. As Europe’s grid faces growing pressure from distributed energy resources (DERs) such as solar, wind, heat pumps and EV chargers, ENSEMBLE™ enables these assets to provide vital frequency and ancillary services traditionally supplied by costly, fossil-fuelled plants. Using private-permissioned blockchain, smart contracts and strong cryptographic safeguards, the platform coordinates DERs in near real time while meeting AML, KYC and GDPR requirements. This reduces transaction costs, increases transparency for regulators, and lowers barriers for clean energy participation. By enhancing grid flexibility and reducing reliance on conventional generation, ENSEMBLE™ supports the European Green Deal and contributes to a more sustainable, resilient and decentralised energy system.

column2: Fair Squares: Affordable Housinf Funding Protocol
column4: Fair Squares introduces a new, community-driven model for financing and managing affordable housing across the EU, where over 40 million people struggle to secure an affordable home. Instead of relying solely on government programmes or large corporate landlords, the protocol enables tenants, investors and housing organisations to collectively shape how housing is funded and managed, ensuring fairness, transparency and long-term affordability. Using a tokenised, digitally distributed funding system, investors own small, traceable shares of a diversified pool of real housing assets. This reduces risk while preventing speculative price inflation, keeping the focus firmly on affordability. Returns are modest and stable, with rent levels influenced by community-determined returns on investment. Fair Squares handles the financial layer so tenants gain secure, affordable housing, housing organisations retain responsibility for property management, and investors participate in a sustainable, socially responsible model. Digital identifiers ensure secure and verifiable ownership and tenancy records. By shifting housing from a profit-driven system to a collaborative, transparent framework, Fair Squares aims to deliver a fairer, more stable and truly affordable housing ecosystem.

column2: Frictionless Markets Sarl (Frictionless Group Holdings Sarl)
column4: Frictionless Markets is reshaping institutional finance by combining traditional systems with advanced on-chain technology. Its OnFi platform enables instant, borderless, multi-currency settlement around the clock, replacing slow, risk-prone traditional processes with transparent and secure DLT-based operations. OnFi converts ERC20 assets and regulated stablecoins into fully backed institutional deposit tokens, supported by strong custody and automated compliance. It also provides access to tokenised money market funds and yield-enhancing “yieldcoins”, offering daily liquidity and competitive returns. The platform includes real-time on-chain FX with an integrated order book, streamlined private-market distribution tools, and ongoing trials for integrated clearing and settlement. Using Chainlink’s decentralised oracle networks, it supports secure multi-chain interoperability and proof-of-reserves. With built-in infrastructure for digital capital raising and on-chain issuance, OnFi delivers a fast, scalable and efficient foundation for next-generation institutional markets.
column11: Executive and legislative organs, financial and fiscal affairs, external affairs.

column2: EMERALD Blockchain-based trustworthiness system
column4: EMERALD provides a user-friendly framework that helps cybersecurity stakeholders manage certifications more efficiently and securely. At its core is a blockchain-based trustworthiness system that allows cloud service providers to guarantee the integrity and transparency of certification evidence while preserving privacy. The system enables evidence collectors to automatically generate proofs of integrity, ensuring that all recorded information is protected against tampering. EMERALD can automatically verify the integrity of stored evidence, while authorised users—such as internal or external auditors—can also perform manual checks through a simple graphical interface. Key features include long-term, tamper-resistant evidence recording using blockchain’s inherent immutability, traceability and verifiability. The platform supports both automated and manual integrity verification, providing a reliable and accessible way to audit cloud service security over time. By combining secure evidence management with a streamlined certification workflow, EMERALD strengthens trust in cloud services and supports more effective cybersecurity assurance.

column2: Blockchain-Based Age Estimation with Privado ID and Privately
column4: Billions Network, Privado ID and Privately have developed a privacy-preserving age-verification solution using self-sovereign identity (SSI), zero-knowledge proofs and verifiable credentials. Traditional age checks often require sharing sensitive personal information, but this approach allows users to prove they are old enough without disclosing data such as their date of birth or ID number, significantly reducing privacy and security risks. The solution is designed for online platforms offering age-restricted services across multiple EU member states. By combining Privado ID’s decentralised identity technology with Privately’s on-device AI age estimation, the system supports secure, cross-border verification while meeting GDPR requirements and aligning with the European Digital Identity Framework. Age credentials are issued by trusted EU entities and stored in a user-controlled identity wallet. Users can then generate a zero-knowledge proof that they meet the required age, with no personal data leaving their device. The system also provides an accountable decision-making mechanism through an AI trust framework, ensuring transparency and regulatory compliance. By minimising data sharing and enhancing user control, this solution offers a harmonised, privacy-first model for age verification across Europe.

column2: Oneboard AG
column4: Oneboard streamlines compliance for financial institutions by providing reusable, auto-updating KYC and KYB profiles. Customers complete verification once, and their compliance data is securely stored and dynamically updated with regulatory changes and document expirations, reducing costs by up to 86%. The platform integrates seamlessly with existing banking systems, using decentralised storage and cryptographic security to ensure privacy, data integrity and cross-jurisdictional compliance. Designed for evolving EU regulations such as MiCA, DORA and AML directives, Oneboard automates workflows, accelerates onboarding, and enables institutions to maintain high compliance standards efficiently.

column2: Parfin – Institutional Blockchain Infrastructure for the European Blockchain Sandbox
column4: Parfin’s Rayls blockchain provides an institutional-grade platform for privacy, compliance, and scalable digital finance. Designed for banks, asset managers, financial market infrastructures and central banks, Rayls enables the issuance of digital currencies, tokenisation of real-world assets, and compliant cross-border transactions. The platform uses zero-knowledge proofs to ensure transaction confidentiality while maintaining regulatory oversight and supports a hub-and-spoke architecture for private subnets, achieving high throughput and reduced costs. Fully aligned with EU regulations, including GDPR, KYC/AML and MiCA, Rayls is interoperable with public and private blockchains, allowing seamless integration of tokenised assets with traditional finance and DeFi. Key applications include wholesale CBDCs, on-chain trading of tokenised securities, bank-issued stablecoins, and compliant cross-border payments. By providing custody, brokerage, stablecoin payments, treasury automation, and RWA tokenisation, Parfin enables financial institutions to modernise operations, enhance liquidity, and bridge traditional finance with digital assets securely and efficiently.
column11: Executive and legislative organs, financial and fiscal affairs, external affairs.

column2: PIXELPAI APS - financial compliance infrastructure platform
column4: PixelPai is a Web3/4 financial compliance infrastructure platform that enables creators and businesses to launch, manage, and monetise blockchain-based models securely and efficiently. The platform combines advanced compliance tools, embedded wallet technology, and a secure digital asset marketplace to lower barriers for Web3 actors while ensuring adherence to regulatory frameworks such as MiCA. PixelPai supports both custodial and non-custodial onboarding, automates financial monitoring, AML compliance, and fraud detection, and facilitates transactions, minting, burning, and trading of NFTs and utility tokens with provable ownership. By integrating agentic AI with multiple blockchains and DLTs, PixelPai optimises asset management and adaptive interactions, providing a full-stack solution for compliant, scalable Web3/4 business models.
column11: Executive and legislative organs, financial and fiscal affairs, external affairs.

column2: Revolutionising home care system by linking verified care work to tokenised finance
column4: CareCredits.io is transforming Europe’s €344 billion home care sector by linking verified care work with tokenised finance, giving care workers utility tokens and enabling investors to support compliant care SMEs through tokenised equity. Powered by LEI-based KYC/AML, ISO-standard vLEI digital identities, and a Market Maker of Last Resort, it opens new financial opportunities while strengthening workforce sustainability. Integrated with scheduling software, its KXCO.io smart contract rewards workers with wrapped utility tokens for every verified hour of care, redeemable via existing supermarket loyalty schemes at no extra cost to employers. These tokens are backed by regulatory-compliant security tokens, bridging digital assets with traditional finance. Global LEI validation and vLEI cryptographic identity verification ensure robust compliance, secure access, and trusted participation across the entire ecosystem.

column2: Blockchain to transform decentralised energy management and distribution
column4: The Renewable Energy Communities (RECs) use case shows how blockchain can transform decentralised energy management and distribution. Using distributed ledger technology, smart contracts, tokenisation, and DAO-based governance, RECs enable households, businesses, and public bodies to co-own and manage local energy assets. This cooperative model improves transparency, efficiency, and community participation while supporting sustainability and aligning with EU climate goals. Key elements include the tokenisation of energy and financial instruments—covering energy offtake, voting rights, membership, ownership, and financing—along with participant wallets that allow members to store, manage, and trade these digital assets securely. Governance is delivered through a DAO, ensuring transparent, inclusive decision-making and giving members direct influence over proposals, resource allocation, and the community’s strategic direction. The model also incorporates strict privacy and regulatory compliance, meeting requirements under EU energy directives, GDPR, the Data Governance Act, MiCAR, MiFID, and relevant taxation rules. The Amelander Energie Coöperatie illustrates this approach, demonstrating how community-driven energy systems, supported by DLT, smart contracts, and tokenised ownership, can enhance local resilience, improve operational transparency, and broaden access to investment for long-term sustainability.

column2: Blockchain to power intelligent, scalable Digital Product Passport (DPP) infrastructure
column4: The Tree Protocol is a decentralised, tokenised knowledge-graph system designed to power intelligent, scalable Digital Product Passport (DPP) infrastructure. Built on blockchain smart contracts, API integrations, and developer SDKs, it enables efficient creation of DPP solutions and seamless cross-platform data sharing. By unlocking high-quality product data, Tree accelerates circular economy models across value chains such as textiles, allowing repair, resale, and recycling services to operate more efficiently and offer improved consumer experiences. Its blockchain-based infrastructure connects brands with circular platforms, turning compliance data into commercial value by supporting EU DPP requirements and enabling lifecycle monetisation. Through its partnership with Vaayu, Tree aldo supports automated traceability data collection via API, ensuring alignment with the EU’s Ecodesign for Sustainable Products Regulation and enabling smooth, compliant data management within the Shopify ecosystem.

column2: Platform to deliver a blockchain-agnostic framework for tokenising real-world ESG assets
column4: Bridge10X's platform delivers a blockchain-agnostic framework for tokenising real-world ESG assets—such as solar farms, geothermal plants, and reforestation projects—into fully compliant, tradable digital securities. Each token is backed by the underlying physical asset, ensuring stability, and is linked to carbon-allowance-backed digital units to enhance liquidity across fragmented markets. Supported by market makers and index listings, the system enables broad investor access while maintaining strong market performance. Bridge10x oversees the asset projects via the tokenforge platform, ensuring professional management throughout the asset lifecycle. AI-driven valuation and smart-contract automation provide transparent pricing, streamlined transactions, and verified environmental impact aligned with global standards. Operating within a regulated, EEA-compliant framework—including AML/KYC, custody, and registrar services—the platform offers secure, seamless participation for both retail and institutional investors seeking reliable, next-generation ESG investment opportunities.

column2: Phase IV AI - Advanced AI Models for Health Data Privacy
column4: PHASE IV AI is a Horizon Europe-funded project focused on developing advanced AI models for the health sector, led by the University of Turku. The project aims to harness AI for data-driven innovation in healthcare, supporting preventive care and clinical decision-making. A key challenge is that health data is often distributed and access-limited, restricting the development of trustworthy AI systems that require large datasets for training and validation. PHASE IV AI addresses this by advancing synthetic data generation methods, developing metrics for testing and validation, and creating protocols that allow multi-party computation—enabling AI model development without accessing real-world data. This approach ensures secure, privacy-compliant data use, unlocking the potential of AI for health technology assessment, education, and wider adoption of AI-powered tools.

column2: Florence's First AI-powered chatbot
column4: The City of Florence has launched FestinaLente, its first generative-AI chatbot, to simplify the process of changing one’s official residence and improve access to municipal services. The chatbot provides clear information on requirements, documents and procedures, helping citizens avoid phone calls, emails or in-person visits. FestinaLente understands natural-language questions and responds in 14 languages, making it accessible to both residents and international users. Available 24/7 on the city’s website, it offers first-level assistance based on official municipal documentation validated by demographic-services staff. Built on a modular AI platform that allows the city to choose among different large-language models, it uses ChatGPT as its main engine. The project was developed by the City of Florence with support from its in-house digital services company, Firenze Smart, and a private technology partner. The chatbot does not replace staff but reduces routine workloads, allowing employees to focus on more complex tasks.

column2: Chatbot to revolutionise tax return analysis
column4: Italy’s Finance Department, through its Economic-Fiscal Studies and Research Directorate, has introduced a new AI-powered chatbot designed to support the analysis of tax returns. The system uses advanced artificial intelligence to process large volumes of fiscal data, enabling faster identification of inconsistencies, patterns, and potential risk indicators across millions of declarations. By automating parts of the review process traditionally carried out manually, the tool aims to reduce processing times, improve accuracy, and allow tax officials to focus on more complex investigative work. This initiative forms part of a broader strategy to modernise tax-administration processes and strengthen data-driven decision-making within Italy’s Ministry of Economy and Finance.
column8: Finance Department's Economic-Fiscal Studies and Research Directorate

column2: German government hotline 115: AI-based chatbot for 24/7 support
column4: Germany’s national public-service hotline “115,” which provides citizens with information and guidance on a wide range of administrative procedures, has upgraded its digital support system by replacing its earlier rule-based chatbot with a new AI-driven version. The previous chatbot could only respond to predefined questions and followed rigid decision trees, often leading to limited or unsatisfactory answers. The new system, powered by modern generative AI, is designed to understand natural language queries far more flexibly and provide more accurate, context-aware responses. This upgrade aims to make interactions with public administration faster, clearer and more user-friendly. Citizens contacting the 115 service — whether online or through supported digital channels — can now receive more relevant information about services such as registrations, permits, public benefits or local administrative procedures. The shift reflects a broader German effort to modernise public-sector communication tools and improve accessibility and efficiency in everyday administrative interactions.

column2: Sovereign AI for Public Administration in France
column4: France has announced a strategic public–private partnership with Mistral AI and SAP SE to develop and deploy a “sovereign AI” infrastructure tailored for public administrations. The goal is to build a secure, European-controlled AI stack — from data centres and cloud infrastructure to enterprise applications — so that governments and public institutions can leverage artificial intelligence without relying on foreign (non-EU) tech providers. The initiative aims to enhance efficiency, transparency and responsiveness of public services in a way that respects data sovereignty and local regulatory standards. By mid-2026 the partners plan to sign a binding agreement, and between 2026 and 2030 deploy concrete AI-based use cases across administrations.

column2: Sovereign AI for Public Administration in Germany
column4: Germany has announced a strategic public–private partnership with Mistral AI and SAP SE to develop and deploy a “sovereign AI” infrastructure tailored for public administrations. The goal is to build a secure, European-controlled AI stack — from data centres and cloud infrastructure to enterprise applications — so that governments and public institutions can leverage artificial intelligence without relying on foreign (non-EU) tech providers. The initiative aims to enhance efficiency, transparency and responsiveness of public services in a way that respects data sovereignty and local regulatory standards. By mid-2026 the partners plan to sign a binding agreement, and between 2026 and 2030 deploy concrete AI-based use cases across administrations.

column2: OpenAI For Greece - ChatGPT Edu in Secondary Education
column4: “OpenAI for Greece” is a newly launched partnership between OpenAI, the government of the Hellenic Republic, Onassis Foundation and Endeavor Greece. The initiative aims to expand access to high‑quality AI tools across two main fronts: education and entrepreneurship. In education, Greece will pilot a version of AI — ChatGPT Edu — in secondary schools, to help teachers integrate AI responsibly, boost productivity, and build AI literacy. In parallel, the programme launches a Greek AI Accelerator to support local AI‑focused start‑ups: selected ventures will receive access to OpenAI technology, credits, mentorship, compliance and scaling guidance, international exposure, and support in sectors aligned with Greece’s national priorities (education, public services, healthcare, climate). The goal is to leverage AI to foster human capital, innovation and economic growth across the country.

column2: OpenAI For Greece - Greek AI Accelerator to support local AI‑focused start‑ups
column4: “OpenAI for Greece” is a newly launched partnership between OpenAI, the government of the Hellenic Republic, Onassis Foundation and Endeavor Greece. The initiative aims to expand access to high‑quality AI tools across two main fronts: education and entrepreneurship. In education, Greece will pilot a version of AI — ChatGPT Edu — in secondary schools, to help teachers integrate AI responsibly, boost productivity, and build AI literacy. In parallel, the programme launches a Greek AI Accelerator to support local AI‑focused start‑ups: selected ventures will receive access to OpenAI technology, credits, mentorship, compliance and scaling guidance, international exposure, and support in sectors aligned with Greece’s national priorities (education, public services, healthcare, climate). The goal is to leverage AI to foster human capital, innovation and economic growth across the country.

column2: Digital Doctor Assistant
column4: The Greek government has launched an AI‑powered tool known as Digital Doctor Assistant, now available to doctors through the national electronic health record system. Through the platform myHealthDoc, physicians can instantly access patients’ medical data, review histories, medications and test results, request summaries or personalized reports using natural‑language queries, and even use voice interaction. The aim is to reduce administrative burden, cut bureaucracy, speed up access to relevant clinical information, and support medical decision‑making within the public health system. Authorities say this marks the first deployment of AI in the country’s public health sector, as part of a broader digital‑health transformation effort.

column2: Greek Government and Sword Health team up for AI-driven initiative
column4: The Greek government has partnered with Sword Health to launch an AI‑driven healthcare initiative that seeks to modernise how citizens access care. The programme will integrate AI into the national health hotline, which is the first point of contact for individuals seeking medical guidance or trying to navigate the healthcare system. By using AI for triage, coordination and user‑support tasks, the system aims to reduce waiting times, relieve pressure on hospitals, increase responsiveness and ensure consistent support across Greece, including rural and remote areas. This project is expected to serve more than 1 million citizens and is part of a broader ambition to build an AI-enhanced national health system, offering a “smart entry” point to care.

column2: City of Częstochowa will become the first in Poland to begin using the Polish Large Language Model (PLLuM)
column4: The city of Częstochowa in Poland has become the first municipality to deploy the national Polish language model PLLuM in local administration. Under a pilot programme in collaboration with the Ministry of Digital Affairs, the AI is being used to support city officials in handling routine administrative tasks more efficiently. This includes drafting official letters, summarising long or complex documents, analysing residents’ inquiries, and providing quick guidance for internal workflows. The implementation aims to reduce processing times, improve transparency in local administration, and free up staff to focus on higher-value tasks. By integrating PLLuM into everyday municipal operations, Częstochowa seeks to modernise local government services while exploring the practical benefits and limitations of generative AI in public administration.

column2: Bilingual AI Chatbot and Mobile Robot
column4: The Life Events Robot is an AI‑powered assistant built to help citizens in Malta with major life‑event procedures — such as registering a birth, marriage or divorce — via a bilingual (Maltese and English) chatbot and, eventually, a mobile robot. It uses a retrieval‑augmented generation (RAG) model combined with the ChatGPT API to give accurate, up‑to‑date guidance based on relevant legal and administrative information. The system supports both text and voice interaction (real‑time transcription, text‑to‑speech), and is designed to provide clear, accessible answers to queries around government services. As a next step, the solution will be deployed on a Temi V3 mobile robot, able to navigate public spaces and provide on‑site, conversational assistance — bridging the gap between online support and real‑world service desks.

column2: Introduction of Microsoft 365 Copilot within the public sector
column4: The Malta Information Technology Agency (MITA) announced that, starting in 2025, the Maltese government will roll out Microsoft 365 Copilot — an AI-powered assistant integrated into Microsoft 365 applications — across the public sector. With a €4 million investment, over 8,000 public-service employees will receive Copilot on their laptops over the next two years and will be trained through a newly established Centre of Excellence to ensure effective adoption. This initiative is part of the national digital governance strategy Vision Malta 2050, aiming to reduce administrative burdens, enhance productivity, and modernise public-service delivery. A notable feature is that Copilot will support the Maltese language, making the tool more accessible to government staff and reinforcing local identity within public administration.

column2: Mon parquet assistant - My assistant prosecutor
column4: Mon parquet assistant is a digital-justice solution, developed under the aegis of beta.gouv.fr and the Parquet général de Paris, aimed at speeding up the processing of criminal-case files in courts under the Cour d'appel de Paris. The tool helps magistrates manage, review and analyse judicial documents more efficiently — by uploading full case files (digital or scanned), automatically detecting the types of offenses, enabling quick searches for relevant information, highlighting key passages, and supporting both a rapid overview and a deeper review of records. The goal is to reduce repetitive, low-value tasks and shorten procedural delays, especially in high-volume or “mass-litigation” situations. The service is still under development.
column8: Ministry of Justice, Paris Public Prosecutor's Office

column2: SNCF’s AI Maintenance Scheduler Reduces Train Downtime by 45%
column4: France’s national rail operator SNCF struggled with unexpected mechanical failures in its TGV fleet, as traditional time-based maintenance often missed early risks while causing unnecessary servicing. This led to inefficiencies, delays, and higher costs. To improve reliability, SNCF deployed an AI-driven predictive maintenance platform that analyses onboard sensor data and external inputs such as weather conditions. Using Bayesian inference and LSTM forecasting models, the system predicts short-term failure risks, prioritizes interventions through visual dashboards, and automatically aligns maintenance tasks with technician schedules and parts availability. The initiative reduced maintenance-related downtime by 45%, added around 68,000 operational hours annually, and improved punctuality to 94% on major routes. Spare-part wastage fell by 19%, technician stress and overtime dropped by 30%, and operating costs were reduced by €28 million over three years. SNCF is expanding the solution to additional fleets and regional services, with plans to integrate smart-station IoT data, enable network-wide optimization, and test generative AI for simulating rare fault scenarios, while exploring international rollout across Europe.

column2: Microsoft Copilot in Cyprus' Public Administration
column4: The Government of Cyprus has begun introducing Microsoft 365 Copilot across the public administration as part of its wider digital modernisation efforts. In the initial phase, 350 civil servants will receive licences and dedicated training to ensure the tool is used safely and effectively. Copilot is intended to support officials by automating routine and time-consuming tasks such as drafting documents, analysing large volumes of data, and managing administrative processes. By reducing manual workload, the initiative aims to improve efficiency and allow staff to focus more on providing high-value services to citizens. This rollout forms part of a broader €5 million “AI in Government” programme designed to expand the use of artificial intelligence within the public sector, including applications related to the labour market, climate challenges, and other areas of state activity.
column8: Ministry of Research, Innovation and Digital Policy

column2: MyMDT - My Medical Digital Twin
column4: MyMDT – My Medical Digital Twin is a platform designed for personalised management of hypertension, combining physiological simulation, machine learning, and data from wearable devices. The project demonstrates that creating clinically meaningful digital twins is a complex and multidisciplinary undertaking, requiring collaboration across multiple research areas. MyMDT employs lumped-parameter models to represent cardiovascular dynamics, together with uncertainty quantification and sensitivity analysis to identify key physiological parameters and guide targeted data collection. These methods improve model personalisation and strengthen clinical relevance. The project also forms part of a wider international digital-twin ecosystem: in the United States, the National Academies of Sciences highlight remaining cross-disciplinary research gaps, while in Europe, the EDITH initiative focuses on clinical adoption. MyMDT is further aligned with the Virtual Physiological Human community, which promotes integrative modelling and provides opportunities for researchers entering the field. Through these combined efforts, MyMDT contributes to the growing role of digital twins in reshaping the future of healthcare.
column8: Norwegian University of Science and Technology

column2: HARMONIE project (Health Assessment Refinement for Mitigating Noise and Air Quality Effects)
column4: HARMONIE describes a new European initiative that uses advanced digital twin technology to help cities reduce air and noise pollution and protect public health. The project, coordinated by the Barcelona Supercomputing Center and involving 19 partners including Fondazione Bruno Kessler (FBK), combines real-world pilot data from cities like Barcelona, Lausanne, Sarajevo, Gävle and Barakaldo, with two types of digital simulations: a Lung Digital Twin that models how pollutants affect the respiratory system, and a Citizen-Centric Digital Twin that maps pollution exposure and supports “what-if” policymaking.
