HARMONIE aims to give policymakers and citizens better tools to design healthier, more equitable and environmentally sustainable urban environments.

column2: HARMONIE project: Lung Digital Twin
column4: HARMONIE is a European research project that leverages digital twin technologies to support healthier, more sustainable, and more equitable cities. By combining environmental data, advanced simulations, and citizen-centric approaches, HARMONIE helps policymakers understand how air and noise pollution affect human health and evaluate the impact of urban planning and policy decisions before implementing them in the real world. Within the project, the digital solution "Lung Digital Twin" focuses on the direct health impacts of air pollution on the human respiratory sytem. It integrates clinical data, environmental exposure information, and physiological models to simulate how pollutants interact with the lungs and contribute to respiratory diseases. This use case helps researchers and health authorities better understand risk factors, assess long-term health effects, and design targeted prevention strategies, particularly for vulnerable populations.

column2: HARMONIE project: Citizen-centric Digital Twin
column4: HARMONIE is a European research project that leverages digital twin technologies to support healthier, more sustainable, and more equitable cities. By combining environmental data, advanced simulations, and citizen-centric approaches, HARMONIE helps policymakers understand how air and noise pollution affect human health and evaluate the impact of urban planning and policy decisions before implementing them in the real world. Within the project, the digital solution "Citizen-Centric Digital Twin" models how individuals and communities are exposed to air and noise pollution within urban environments. By combining mobility patterns, environmental monitoring data, and urban infrastructure information, it enables “what-if” simulations of policy scenarios such as traffic restrictions, green infrastructure, or zoning changes. This use case supports evidence-based decision-making, helping cities reduce pollution exposure, improve quality of life, and promote more inclusive and sustainable urban development.

column2: ComparAI - the chatbot arena
column4: compar:IA is a French government-led digital platform designed to compare conversational AI models and educate citizens about generative AI. Developed within a state start-up initiative under the Ministry of Culture and integrated into Beta.gouv.fr, the platform allows users to interact with two anonymised AI models, evaluate their responses, and vote on the best output. These votes feed into a public dataset that helps improve AI models for the French language while respecting linguistic and cultural diversity. The platform serves both the general public and AI experts, providing a space to test, measure, and compare AI models, explore their characteristics, and use the data for research or educational purposes. It also helps raise awareness of AI limitations, biases, and ethical considerations, making AI more transparent and accessible. Additionally, the platform is expanding to other European countries, including Lithuania, Sweden, and Denmark, to support multilingual AI development.

column2: Daisy - an AI "granny" that will waste scammers' time
column4: Daisy, a non-human “AI granny,” has been developed by the UK telecom operator Virgin Media O2 as an innovative tool to disrupt phone scammers. Her role is to waste scammers’ time by engaging them in long, rambling conversations — chatting about family, knitting, cats, and responding with confusing answers — preventing fraudsters from reaching real victims. Powered by multiple AI models, Daisy listens and replies in real time, convincingly imitating an elderly and vulnerable person. During testing, she has managed to keep scammers on the line for up to 40 minutes. The initiative aims not only to reduce the success rate of scam attempts, but also to raise public awareness of how fraudsters operate, demonstrating how AI can be used creatively to protect citizens.

column2: mAIGreece - the digital AI assistant for visitors
column4: The mAiGreece platform is a digital AI assistant launched by the Greek Ministry of Tourism in collaboration with the Ministry of Digital Governance to enhance services for visitors to Greece. The application provides comprehensive information on hospitals, health centres, embassies and consulates, police stations, Blue Flag beaches, museums, and content from visitgreece.gr, along with frequently asked questions. It is available in 31 languages, including major European, Asian, and Middle Eastern languages, ensuring accessibility for a wide range of international visitors. mAiGreece also includes safety features, such as the ability to send an SMS with the user’s geographical location to the Ministry of Civil Protection’s operations centre via the 112 emergency number for immediate assistance. The platform combines AI-driven query handling and multilingual support to provide tourists with fast, accurate information, improve their experience, and strengthen safety and accessibility across Greece.

column2: AmigaAI - Chatbot for the legal sector
column4: The GesetzeFinden.at Legal AI project is Austria’s first AI model specifically designed for the legal sector. Announced in September 2024, the initiative is led by the Vienna-based Legal-Tech start-up GesetzeFinden.at in collaboration with the Psychological Faculty of Sigmund Freud Private University (SFU), the law firm CERHA HEMPEL, and Fraunhofer Austria. The project aims to develop a legal-specific large language model, AmigaAI, capable of understanding and interpreting Austrian legal language to improve access to legal information and enhance efficiency for legal professionals. The initiative addresses the complexity and specialised language of the legal domain, which makes it challenging for both laypeople and professionals to navigate. Using a novel “Society of Minds” approach, multiple AI chatbots collaborate internally to answer user queries, producing more accurate results while optimising computational resources. Funded by the Austrian Research Promotion Agency (FFG), the project is scheduled for implementation over 24 months and focuses on combining technological innovation with legal and psychological expertise to create a reliable, user-friendly, and EU AI Act-compliant tool. AmigaAI will serve both legal professionals and the general public, aiming to improve access to law, streamline legal workflows, and provide a practical example of AI application in the Austrian legal sector.

column2: JUANO - app to anonymise court decisions for publication on the justice internet portal
column4: JUANO is an application that helps anonymise court decisions for publication on the justice internet portal. JUANO is based on an artificial intelligence engine that recognises entities to be anonymised and automatically suggests categories based on the text. This application ensures the consistency of the anonymisation of court decisions and facilitates the process of making them available to lawyers and other interested parties in compliance with data protection regulations.

column2: AI in Rectruitment Procedures
column4: The State Centre for Human Resources and Organisation Management intends to improve recruitment procedures by using artificial intelligence in order to assist their agents in checking the conditions of admissibility of applications and registrations for the general aptitude test for the Luxembourg civil service.
column8: State Centre for Human Resources and Organisation Management
column11: General economic, commercial and labour affair

column2: Automatic labelisation of documents
column4: The Central legislative service aims to automatically label (categorise/classify) documents based on their content, with a view to coupling a chatbot with the knowledge graph of Luxembourgish legislation.
column11: Executive and legislative organs, financial and fiscal affairs, external affairs.

column2: Outlier detection - a system for detecting aberrant data
column4: The Ministry of Home Affairs aims to set up automated monitoring using a system for detecting aberrant data (outlier detection) in the financial data of the municipal sector.
column11: Executive and legislative organs, financial and fiscal affairs, external affairs.

column2: Machine learning for statistics
column4: The National Institute of statistics and economic studies (STATEC) will implement a data science and machine learning program to enable the production of statistics on the mobile telephone database.
column8: National Institute of statistics and economic studies (STATEC)

column2: ADEM Interact - AI conversational platform for the Employment Agency
column4: ADEM Interact is an AI conversational platform that will assist state agents of the Employment Agency ADEM who receive messages from job seekers, by formulating personalised responses. This is expected to apply to emails and enquiries received via a new Chatbot service integrated into the ADEM website.
column11: General economic, commercial and labour affair

column2: ADEM Profiling
column4: ADEM Profiling will allow through the analysis of existing data at ADEM to develop a support model for their agents to assess which jobseekers need which measures and level of support to access employment. This diagnostic is possible by taking into consideration the characteristics and professional profile of the jobseeker.
column11: General economic, commercial and labour affair

column2: Anomaly detection: machine learning to detect water leaks
column4: The 'Anomaly detection' project is designed to enable the local authority association, DEA (Distribution d'Eau des Ardennes), to use machine learning to detect water leaks in the network and identify changes in a set of data with daily and seasonal differences. The aim of this project is to increase the monitoring capabilities of the DEA, who is responsible for producing and distributing water to 29 municipalities in Luxembourg.

column2: Assitance in drafting judgments JUAIDE
column4: This project concerns an artificial intelligence component to assist judges in drafting legal documents. It analyses the context of the text being drafted in real time and automatically suggests relevant legal texts and court decisions. This system shall significantly reduce the time spent on legal research, while improving the accuracy and quality of the references used. By facilitating immediate access to applicable legal information, it will optimise the drafting process and enhance the efficiency of the judicial process.

column2: UrbIA - AI for cadastral data
column4: The Land Registry and Topography Administration is responsible for managing the country's cadastral data. This data has been systematically organised for 200 years, both in graphic form (cadastral plans and sketch maps) and in tables recording changes in property rights. Information on property rights was recorded by hand in registers structured in tables from 1824 to 1972. The objective of this project is to compare different approaches to interpreting some of these registers for a given municipality, identify the most appropriate method, and then implement it within the ACT.

column2: "Sproocherkennung fir d'Lëtzebuergescht": improving schreifmaschinn.lu, an AI-based speech-to-text tool in various languages
column4: The Zenter fir d'Lëtzebuerger Sprooch (ZLS – Center for Luxembourgish Language) has developed the prototype schreifmaschinn.lu, an AI-based speech-to-text tool in various languages, including Luxembourgish. The project ‘Sproocherkennung fir d'Lëtzebuergescht’ is expanding the current hypersynchronised text and audio dataset from 55 hours to 80 hours to improve the performance of schreifmaschinn.lu. The aim is to meet two requirements: diversification of speakers and situations on the one hand, and spelling accuracy on the other.
column8: Zenter fir d'Lëtzebuerger Sprooch (ZLS – Centre for Luxembourgish Language)

column2: "Iwwersetzungskorpus fir d'Lëtzebuergescht" - multilingual parallel corpus
column4: The Zenter fir d'Lëtzebuerger Sprooch (ZLS – Center for Luxembourgish Language) is proposing the establishment of a multilingual parallel corpus. This corpus will focus on Luxembourgish as the source language — and therefore the main language — with translations into three target languages: German, French and English. This multilingual parallel corpus aims to directly address the current lack of high-quality multilingual datasets focused on Luxembourgish. It will be a valuable resource for supporting advanced machine translation, training artificial intelligence language models, computational linguistics research, and other technological applications, both in Luxembourg and internationally.
column8: Zenter fir d'Lëtzebuerger Sprooch (ZLS – Centre for Luxembourgish Language)

column2: AI Reader - enhancing the efficiency of data entry processes
column4: The project led by the Commissariat aux affaires maritimes (CAM – Commission for Maritime Affairs) aims to develop a tool capable of automatically extracting predefined data from official documents, such as visas, in PDF (text or image) or JPEG format without human intervention. The tool is designed to enhance the efficiency of data entry processes for the Seafarers’ service by minimising the number of data points required from customers, reducing errors during initial data entry, limiting the need for verification and correction by CAM agents, and enabling automatic integration of the extracted data into the relevant systems.
column8: Commissariat aux affaires maritimes (CAM – Commission for Maritime Affairs)

column2: AI4DI - AI-Powered Automation of Information Requests
column4: The National Commission for Data Protection’s (CNPD) "Information Requests" service handles a diverse range of inquiries received through email, mail, and telephone. As the number of questions and responses has grown over time, manual searches by staff have become increasingly time-consuming. This project seeks to automate both the retrieval of relevant information and the creation of preliminary draft responses using the existing database. The proposed solution involves developing an AI-powered tool based on a large language model (LLM) that can efficiently identify pertinent information and generate suitable responses.

column2: Legal and normative text analyser
column4: The Luxembourg Office of Accreditation and Surveillance (OLAS) aims to develop a tool, the ‘Legal and Normative Text Analyser’, designed to analyse documentation from recognised and reliable sources such as Legilux, EUR-Lex, ISO, CEN, EA, ILAC, IAF, as well as locally stored files. The tool will identify and collect relevant documents or information demonstrating compliance with the requirements applicable to the accreditation of specific activities, analyse their content to extract the necessary requirements for accrediting conformity assessment bodies, and generate detailed reports based on the analysis results.
column8: Luxembourg Office of Accreditation and Surveillance (OLAS)
column11: Executive and legislative organs, financial and fiscal affairs, external affairs.

column2: ScreenreaderLB - AI-Powered Luxembourgish Accessibility for the Visually Impaired
column4: The ScreenreaderLB solution, developed by the Government Information and Press Service, addresses the increasing need for accessible content in Luxembourgish for visually impaired users. Currently, the absence of speech synthesis technology for this language requires blind or visually impaired individuals to rely on screen readers in German or other languages. ScreenreaderLB aims to create a screen reader specifically for Luxembourgish, providing smooth and accurate speech synthesis to allow visually impaired users to interact more effectively with digital content, enhancing both their browsing experience and access to information.

column2: Digitalising key administrative processes using blockchain technology in the Municipality of Chieti
column4: Mangrovia Blockchain Solutions, in collaboration with A Software Factory, is supporting the Municipality of Chieti in digitalising key administrative processes using blockchain technology. The project integrates a distributed ledger to enhance transparency, data integrity, and automation through smart contracts, allowing citizens’ information to be accessed “once only” while ensuring full compliance with privacy regulations. Initial pilot processes include maternity allowances, elderly home care, disability services, and socio-health contributions, chosen for their social impact and the potential benefits of blockchain transparency. Following a successful pilot, the initiative plans to extend blockchain use to procurement and accounts payable processes, improving efficiency, traceability, and citizen services across the municipality.

column2: Blockchain Pilot for Transparent Food Redistribution in Cosenza
column4: The Municipality of Cosenza, in partnership with NTT DATA, the University of Calabria, and Banco Alimentare, is launching a pioneering national pilot to redistribute near-expiry food from supermarkets and other retailers to families in need. The project leverages blockchain technology to certify donations, ensuring transparency, traceability, and data immutability, while enabling tax incentives for contributing businesses. The University of Calabria developed a mathematical model powering the platform, which calculates appropriate rewards for donors based on product characteristics and the potential benefit to recipients. Banco Alimentare manages the final storage and distribution of food to those in need. The initiative aims to reduce food waste, enhance resource availability for non-profits, and provide a clear picture of local social needs. By combining blockchain, innovative business models, and collaboration among public and private stakeholders, the project establishes a transparent, efficient, and socially impactful system for welfare distribution.

column2: Italy’s First Green Blockchain Municipality
column4: Borgo d’Anaunia, a small town of 2,500 in northern Italy, has converted its century-old municipal hydroelectric plant to partly power blockchain operations, marking a first for Italy and one of the first examples in Europe. Partnering with local start-up Alps Blockchain, the municipality will sell both electricity and computational power, using 40 supercomputers for crypto mining while maintaining grid supply. The initiative aims to increase revenue—expected to rise by 40% from one-fifth of energy output—and promote sustainable blockchain practices, utilising renewable hydroelectric energy. The system automatically switches off miners when water flow is insufficient, ensuring adaptability.

column2: Blockchain for waste management
column4: Miglianico, a small town of 4,900 in Chieti, has become the first municipality in the world to integrate blockchain into waste management. Using an RFID-based system enhanced with blockchain technology, the town tracks individual waste contributions per capita, improving transparency, reducing costs, and rewarding virtuous citizens. Since adopting the system, separate waste collection reached nearly 85%, cutting municipal expenses from €900,000 to €660,000. Citizens receive RFID-tagged bins and can track their contributions via wearable devices, with rewards such as free daily water to encourage proper recycling. Blockchain ensures the data is certified, secure, and immutable, providing a reliable record of waste contributions, optimising tax calculations, and enabling insights into household waste patterns. Developed by Italian tech company Partitalia Srl, the initiative demonstrates how blockchain can deliver measurable economic, environmental, and social benefits in public service management.

column2: Blockchain-based platform for sustainable tourism and local commerce in mountain communities
column4: BLOCCHI is a blockchain-based platform developed by the University of Turin to promote sustainable tourism and local commerce in mountain communities. Integrating blockchain with an interactive map (FirstLife), it enables tokenisation to create incentives, loyalty programs, and rewards for visiting lesser-known locations and purchasing local products. Using the CommonsHood wallet app, tourists can collect tokens, redeem coupons via QR codes, and earn badges for completing circuits of local businesses. Local businesses and municipalities can create promotional coupons and track engagement. The project is part of NODES, funded by Italy’s PNRR and the EU, aiming to strengthen innovation ecosystems and local leadership in research and development.

column2: Blockchain for smart working
column4: The Municipality of Bari, in partnership with Fincons Group, has launched a pilot Smart Working project as part of the EU-funded H2020 CITADEL initiative. The project introduces agile working for municipal employees, combining office and remote work with flexible hours, while ensuring GDPR-compliant data management. Using Ethereum blockchain and advanced encryption, the platform guarantees secure, reliable, and traceable management of work data. This initiative aims to improve public administration efficiency, employee work-life balance, and citizen-focused digital services, serving as a model for innovative ICT adoption in local governments.
column11: General economic, commercial and labour affair

column2: PROMPT - Narrative Intelligence for Information Integrity
column4: The project PROMPT (Predictive Research on Misinformation & Propagation Trajectories) is a European‑level initiative aiming to combat online disinformation and manipulative narratives. It uses advanced AI — including large language models (LLMs) — combined with network‑analysis techniques to monitor, map and analyse how misleading stories spread across media, social networks and platforms. The initiative tracks narratives around topics like the war in Ukraine, gender issues (LGBTQI+), and elections, in multiple languages. Its goal is not only to detect false or harmful content, but to understand the rhetorical and structural patterns behind manipulation — who spreads it, how it spreads, and why it resonates — and make these insights available through a public dashboard and reports.
column47: Consortium (research institutions, private sector and other PAs)

column2: Provincia IA – Structured Roadmap for Developing a Provincial Artificial Intelligence Strategy in the Province of Castellón
column4: The Castellón Provincial Council, consistently attuned to emerging challenges, seeks to position itself as a leader in shaping the legal, technological, and social framework that will support the responsible and orderly use of artificial intelligence as a driver of modernisation across the entire province. Its ambition is to enhance residents’ quality of life and create new opportunities through a technology which, far from belonging to the future, has already become a defining frontier for public administrations. To this end, a structured Roadmap is being developed to identify all the necessary elements for the sequential creation of a comprehensive and sustainable strategy for the deployment of artificial intelligence (AI) in the province of Castellón. This strategy will draw upon the potential of e-government, open government, and public-sector innovation as catalysts for transformation. It also aims to significantly improve public services and increase the efficiency of the Provincial Council as an organisation, making use of the new opportunities enabled by AI while establishing a framework of trust and safeguards against potential risks. The Roadmap must, by its nature, be organised into several phases, each of which will progress in line with regulatory and technological developments, ensuring consistency, security, and certainty in both its evolution and implementation. Naturally, some phases of the document may advance in parallel. The Public Administration and Innovation Service will coordinate all actions related to artificial intelligence within the Castellón Provincial Council and will be responsible for defining, promoting, and evaluating every phase of the Roadmap.

column2: AI-powered virtual assistant for payments
column4: The PagoPA AI support project is an initiative launched in February 2025 by PagoPA S.p.A., the Italian public company behind the national digital payment platform pagoPA and related citizen services such as the IO app. As part of this effort, PagoPA partnered with Spitch, a leader in conversational AI, to develop an AI-powered virtual assistant designed to improve how citizens receive assistance about the status of payments made through the pagoPA system. The project connects PagoPA’s multiple information sources with Spitch’s orchestration layer to power an omnichannel experience that integrates machine learning modules, natural language understanding systems and large language models, delivering real-time, contextual responses without users needing to wait for a human operator. The solution operates 24/7, scales efficiently during peak times, respects privacy and lays the groundwork for future enhancements like chatbot integration across web and mobile channels.

column2: Sof-IA: management of online medical appointments
column4: Sof-IA is the proprietary artificial intelligence developed by iDoctors, launched publicly in late 2025. Designed to transform how patients and clinicians use the platform, Sof-IA helps users quickly identify the most suitable medical specialist by analysing natural-language descriptions of symptoms, needs, preferred timing, location and budget. This allows it to provide fast, personalised recommendations without performing diagnoses or suggesting treatments. In addition to assisting patients, Sof-IA is integrated into the tools used by the approximately 12.000 doctors on the iDoctors platform. It supports clinicians by simplifying the management of appointments, availability, messaging and administrative tasks — including the ability to perform many actions via voice commands. The aim is to streamline routine processes and make both appointment booking and practice management more intuitive and efficient.

column2: Saro: AI-powered educational digital assistant
column4: Saro is an AI-powered educational assistant being introduced into schools and universities in Romania through a partnership between Digital Nation and the Romanian Ministry of Education and Research. It’s designed to help students, pupils, and teachers by enhancing motivation, reducing bureaucracy, and supporting personalised learning experiences. The initiative is part of a broader effort to integrate artificial intelligence into the national education system, with early pilots showing increased assignment completion and engagement.
column8: Romanian Ministry of Education and Research

column2: Similis: for the identification of similar and identical amendments
column4: The IT Department of the Italian Republic Senate has developed Similis, a system for clustering similar and identical parliamentary amendments, in close collaboration with the Institute of Legal Informatics and Judicial Systems of the National Research Council (CNR-IGSG). Similis is designed to support legislative processes by automatically identifying amendments that share highly similar wording, helping to reduce duplication and improve the efficiency of parliamentary analysis. The system focuses on syntactic similarity rather than semantic interpretation, favouring amendments with closely matching sentence structures and phrasing. It operates without relying on any prior metadata or predefined information about the amendments. Instead, amendments are grouped using hierarchical agglomerative clustering (HAC) with complete linkage. Each amendment is represented as a vector generated through a bag-of-words model with term frequency (TF) weighting and Euclidean normalisation, following standard pre-processing steps such as stemming and stop-word removal. Similarity between amendments is calculated using a cosine similarity metric, with empirically determined thresholds of 0.8 for cosine similarity and 0.2 for dendrogram cut-off. Integrated into a broader suite of digital legislative tools developed by the Senate, Similis complements other AI-supported systems for amendment organisation, classification, and analysis, contributing to more transparent, structured, and efficient law-making processes.

column2: AI Mobility Challenge - DataTalks
column4: DataTalks is an AI-driven service designed to make city information intuitive and useful for residents and visitors alike. By connecting to varied data interfaces, it enables users to ask natural language questions — for example, whether there’s parking space available at a particular location and if weather conditions require an umbrella — and instantly receive real-time answers that integrate parking and meteorological data. This bridges the gap between complex urban datasets and everyday decision-making, helping people plan journeys smarter and reduce needless driving.
column47: Consortium (research institutions, private sector and other PAs)

column2: AI Mobility Challenge - CamTalks
column4: CamTalks is an innovative solution that uses edge computing to interpret live camera feeds on site without sending raw images to the cloud. Users can make simple queries like “how many people are in this image?” and receive quick responses generated by localised AI models. By processing video data at the source and focusing on metadata rather than identifiable footage, CamTalks enhances situational awareness while preserving privacy — a key benefit for smart cities seeking to balance data use with citizens’ rights.
column47: Consortium (research institutions, private sector and other PAs)

column2: AI Mobility Challenge - 0Flow
column4: 0Flow tackles a core challenge in urban logistics: under-utilised transport capacity that leads to unnecessary trips and increased emissions. This solution proposes AI and data-driven optimisation of freight loads and routes, increasing utilisation rates and reducing the number of empty or inefficient trips. By fine-tuning logistics operations, 0Flow helps make goods movement within the city more sustainable, lowering environmental impact while improving operational performance.
column47: Consortium (research institutions, private sector and other PAs)

column2: AI Mobility Challenge - Traffic Info
column4: Traffic Info introduces a multilingual AI system that delivers traffic updates and announcements in natural language — both spoken and written forms. This enhances communication with residents and visitors from diverse backgrounds, ensuring easier access to real-time mobility information. By supporting multiple languages and intuitive formats, the tool improves user experience and helps individuals make informed travel decisions, contributing to smoother and more efficient traffic flows.
column47: Consortium (research institutions, private sector and other PAs)

column2: AI Mobility Challenge - Dynamik Allocator
column4: Developed by Paavo Kytölä and Anita Kuitunen, Dynamik Allocator focuses on optimising the daily routes of mobile workers — such as service technicians or field staff — to minimise redundant driving. By using AI to organise schedules and job sequences more efficiently, it helps lower fuel use and emissions associated with work-related travel. This intelligent allocation of tasks not only cuts environmental impact but also saves time and costs for organisations.
column47: Consortium (research institutions, private sector and other PAs)

column2: Processing of personal data, AI translation service on the Hello Espoo website
column4: The City of Espoo is testing an AI-powered text and voice translation service on its Hello Espoo website from September 2025 to August 2026 to make content available in 13 languages and improve multilingual access to public services. The system collects usage data such as time of use, pages visited, and web domain (IP address), but this data cannot be linked to individual users. Data is used to implement, test, develop and improve the service, and feedback submitted via a form helps enhance accessibility. Usage data will be deleted by the end of the trial (no later than 31 August 2026) and feedback form responses are stored for 30 days. The City of Espoo and its service provider Voice Intuitive Oy process the data in compliance with GDPR, and no data is transferred outside the EU/EEA. Users retain their rights under EU data protection law.
column47: Consortium (research institutions, private sector and other PAs)

column2: Pioneering Europe’s Future of Autonomous Urban Mobility
column4: Turin and the Piedmont region are positioning themselves at the forefront of autonomous driving in Europe. Their dossier to the European Commission proposes that Turin become a city-laboratory for testing AI-powered vehicles, leveraging the region’s strong automotive sector, research institutions like the Politecnico di Torino, and advanced urban infrastructure. The plan envisions real-world trials of self-driving taxis and shuttles on public roads, including monitoring traffic flows with AI systems. The goal is for Turin to join the EU network of pilot cities and, by 2027, offer public mobility services with autonomous vehicles, becoming a model for other European cities in urban mobility innovation.

column2: Piedmont harnesses AI to bring healthcare to every corner
column4: Piedmont is seeking to transform healthcare delivery through AI, with a focus on diagnostics, organisational efficiency, and telemedicine. With over 40% of its territory being mountainous, the region is well-suited for remote healthcare solutions, including telemedicine cabins where patients can undergo medical exams with a trained operator present, while doctors connect remotely via video. AI systems would assist in diagnostics, patient monitoring, and optimising healthcare workflows, ensuring that even residents in remote or underserved areas have access to high-quality, timely medical care. This initiative exemplifies the potential of AI to enhance territorial health coverage and bridge gaps in healthcare accessibility.

column2: ChatPat - Virtual assistant
column4: Chat PaT is a virtual assistant developed by the Autonomous Province of Trento to help citizens, businesses, professionals and other users easily navigate and access the Province’s public services through a simple chat interface. It allows users to ask questions in natural language and receive immediate answers and direct links to official information and online forms from the Province’s Service Catalogue, without needing to know technical or bureaucratic terms. The assistant supports multiple languages (Italian, English, German, French, Spanish and Portuguese), is available 24/7, and is designed to complement (not replace) traditional contact channels, reducing search time and improving user experience. The service uses official provincial data, anonymises interactions to protect privacy under GDPR, and is continuously improved using user feedback. Chat PaT was created as part of the Province’s “Digitalisation of the Public Administration – Citizens and Digital Businesses” Flagship Project funded under the National Complementary Plan to the PNRR, with the aim of simplifying access to digital public services and enhancing usability.

column2: EDF Leverages AI to Predict Grid Stress During Heatwaves
column4: France’s electrical grid is increasingly strained during summer heatwaves, and EDF’s traditional monitoring was largely reactive, often too late to prevent local outages. To address this, EDF deployed an AI system combining transformer-based forecasting and graph neural networks to model high-voltage and regional grids. It ingests weather forecasts, consumption patterns, transformer loads, and local events to simulate thermal stress and alert operators up to 72 hours in advance. A second module recommends demand-shedding strategies, including smart appliance coordination, industrial load adjustments, and battery dispatch, visualised on GIS-enhanced dashboards. In summer 2024, the system prevented three rolling blackouts in Rhône-Alpes, cut response times from 12 to under 3 minutes, reduced household outages by 38%, and lowered transformer degradation by 26%, while improving voltage stability. EDF plans to scale the platform nationwide by 2026, integrate it with Linky smart meters for hyper-local load management, incorporate CO₂ optimisation, and pilot customer incentives for demand response, with potential for cross-border EU grid coordination.

column2: Paris Hospitals Use AI for ICU Bed Allocation and Mortality Prediction
column4: ICU coordination in Paris’s public hospitals struggled with outdated availability data, exposed during COVID surges. Hospitals implemented an AI system using federated learning, allowing predictive models without centralising sensitive data. LSTM networks forecast ICU occupancy from lab results, comorbidities, ventilator use, and environmental factors, while a triage module ranks patients by survival probability. Integration with ER software and ambulance dispatch enables coordinated patient transfers. The system cut ICU transfer times by 58%, tripled planning accuracy, and achieved 92% mortality prediction precision, supporting ethical decision-making and improving allocation of beds, staff, and ventilators. Families received clearer prognostic information. Expansion plans cover trauma, cardiology, and surgical recovery units across 40 hospitals, with anonymised model sharing under the EU Health Data Space, real-time equipment monitoring, automated billing, and clinical oversight to override AI when necessary.

column2: AI to notify the police about suspicious persons
column4: In Zakarpattia, Ukraine, authorities have started using artificial intelligence to support police monitoring of suspicious activity. Video cameras across the region are connected to an AI system that analyses live footage and identifies people behaving unusually, especially near strategic infrastructure. When the AI detects a person it cannot recognise or flags potentially concerning behaviour, it automatically alerts the police, who then assess and take appropriate action such as questioning or detaining individuals. The system is part of a regional security centre and is being expanded with additional detection points at rail stations to improve public safety.

column2: Gismap AI model for land cover analysis
column4: The Gismap solution, implemented by the Ballerup Municipality in 2025, enables the generation of land cover data using an AI model, editing tools, and integration with various environmental datasets. The solution is applied for purposes such as biodiversity registration, operational optimisation, ESG reporting, strategic planning, and spatial planning. The platform is built on publicly available data sources, including the Danish Climate Data Agency, GeoDanmark, the Danish Environmental Portal, and the Ministry of Environment.
column47: Consortium (research institutions, private sector and other PAs)

column2: Implementation of AI in the knowledge database in Topdesk
column4: Ebbot is an AI chatbot, implemented by Randers Municipality, and integrated into TOPdesk, the municipality’s ITSM system. It is available to municipal employees and can answer questions related to IT, accounting, and procurement. Ebbot searches previous cases and knowledge articles to provide answers, and if it cannot find a solution, it assists the user in creating a support ticket.

column2: Roboref - Generative AI in the Randers Municipality
column4: Roboref is an AI tool that automatically records, transcribes, and generates summaries and notes from meetings using generative AI. It is used for both internal and external meetings in the public sector, freeing up time for other work while enhancing the objectivity of documentation. Currently, a select group of employees in Randers Municipality are testing Roboref for internal meetings.

column2: Copilot in the Randers Municipality
column4: All employees in Randers Municipality have been given access to use Copilot in Edge, Microsoft’s generative AI assistant integrated into the Edge browser to support everyday work tasks. Copilot helps with generating and summarizing text, drafting communications, and assisting with routine productivity tasks. This deployment in Randers reflects a broader trend in public administration: many municipalities and regional authorities across the Netherlands and other local governments are adopting Microsoft 365 Copilot to support staff in writing texts, creating summaries, preparing for meetings, and improving overall productivity. Examples include implementations in the Municipality of Arnhem, Breda, Almere, Zaanstad, and other regional bodies, where Copilot is already in use as a generative AI tool integrated with Microsoft 365 applications to support employees in their daily work.

column2: OS2ai - Open-source AI solution in Danish municipalities
column4: Randers Municipality participates in OS2ai, an open-source AI solution developed collaboratively by Aarhus Municipality and several other Danish municipalities. The solution makes it easy to integrate artificial intelligence into municipal workflows and automate tasks across different areas of responsibility. OS2ai is flexible, scalable, and can be used for both daily tasks and specialized AI applications. The platform is built on the foundation established by Aarhus Municipality and is further developed within the OS2 community. More than 80 municipalities and other public sector actors are already participating.

column2: RandersGPT
column4: RandersGPT is an internal GPT for municipal employees, based on OpenAI in Azure. Its purpose is to provide employees with a more secure alternative to Copilot in Edge and ChatGPT. RandersGPT is used to assist with administrative tasks and is accessed through the municipality’s AI portal, where it is set up as an agent that can direct users to specialized assistants also available on the AI portal.

column2: Fasit Dialogstøtte – AI Assistant for Conversation Notes
column4: An AI assistant, integrated into the Fasit case management system, supports caseworkers by automatically transcribing and summarising conversations with citizens, producing draft minutes and journal notes. By handling routine documentation tasks, the assistant frees up time for caseworkers, enabling them to focus on professional decision-making and more complex aspects of their work. The system also helps ensure consistency, accuracy, and completeness in record-keeping, thereby enhancing overall case management quality across the municipality.

column2: Pluto – Road Analysis and Maintenance
column4: Pluto Technologies has developed an AI-based solution that identifies issues with the municipality’s roads using smartphones and GPS, for example, devices installed on municipal refuse trucks. The tool automatically detects damage and wear on road surfaces, eliminating the need for staff to manually collect and record this information. The collected data is visualised on a digital map, enabling more effective and targeted planning of repairs. The solution can help ensure roads are repaired in a timely manner, potentially extending their lifespan by up to 30%. Additionally, the system supports resource prioritisation, provides an overview of heavily used areas, and generates analyses for preventive maintenance.

column2: InDialog Voicebot – Internal AI Support for ESDH and Topdesk
column4: InDialog Voicebot allows employees to quickly obtain answers and access guidance via voice commands, streamlining internal processes and freeing up time for more complex tasks. The solution is currently in the testing phase and is used internally to support guidance within the municipality’s ESDH system and Topdesk.

column2: KAILA - AI tool for Danish legislation
column4: KAILA is an AI tool used to assist legal staff in handling complex legal tasks more efficiently. It can help with drafting legal documents, gathering relevant sources and arguments, providing new insights, and preparing for meetings. When used, KAILA always provides answers based on current Danish legislation and includes direct references to sources in Karnov, enabling legal staff to quickly and easily verify the information.
column11: Executive and legislative organs, financial and fiscal affairs, external affairs.

column2: AI-for-Søren: AI for work injuries
column4: AI-for-Søren is an AI assistant that answers questions related to work-related and eye injuries. It communicates the municipality’s procedures for handling such cases and provides guidance on rights and responsibilities, while also linking to supplementary information from Danish state authorities, including the Danish Working Environment Authority and the National Board of Industrial Injuries, as well as other relevant professional sources. The assistant serves as a practical tool for reporting parties and injured employees, while also helping to streamline workflows for those responsible for managing occupational injury cases within the municipality. It is intended for use by managers, working environment representatives, employees who have experienced a work-related injury, and staff within HR, the MED organisation, Gentofte Working Environment, and the municipality’s occupational injury function. The purpose of the project is to help employees and managers more easily access accurate guidance and ensure that work-related and eye injuries are handled correctly and in compliance with applicable rules and procedures.

column2: 5G launch in Lviv
column4: Kyivstar, Ukraine's largest mobile operator, plans to launch its first commercial fifth-generation (5G) service in Lviv after completing pilot tests. Speaking at the Spring Business Forum, Business Development Director Kostiantyn Vechir confirmed the move, noting that previous 5G tests had been conducted in Kyiv. The launch is part of a coordinated effort with the Ministry of Digital Transformation, as Kyivstar works alongside state authorities and the telecommunications industry to implement a nationwide 5G transition plan.
column47: Consortium (research institutions, private sector and other PAs)

column2: A Sovereign AI Model: National LLM to Enter Beta in 2026
column4: Ukraine is developing its own sovereign large language model (LLM)—a national artificial intelligence system tailored to the Ukrainian language, culture and public-sector needs. The project has moved from concept to technical planning and is being built in partnership with Kyivstar, with the first beta version due in spring 2026. It uses Google’s open Gemma models as a base, which are being adapted and trained on extensive Ukrainian-language datasets drawn from government, academia, media and other sources. An independent expert panel is shaping quality, safety and linguistic benchmarks, while a legal framework for data use and intellectual-property compliance is under development. Once tested, the model will be made available to public institutions, researchers and developers, and is intended to bolster Ukraine’s digital sovereignty and reduce reliance on foreign AI technologies.

column2: ELLI - Chatbot of the City of Linz
column4: The City of Linz provides an AI-powered chatbot service called “ELLI” (short for Elektronische Linzerin) to help citizens find information about municipal services quickly and easily. ELLI is available 24/7 online and answers questions about topics like administrative services, opening hours, civic procedures, local information and more, using natural language understanding to interpret queries and point users to relevant details or external resources. The chatbot supports multilingual interaction, is continuously updated with new content, and helps reduce routine inquiries to the City’s Teleservice Center by providing instant responses to common questions.

column2: Innovative Mathematics Learning Platform (Grades 5–12)
column4: This AI-powered mathematics platform supports students in grades 5–12 by providing curriculum-aligned exercises, intelligent feedback, and adaptive learning paths. It helps students strengthen mathematical understanding, practice problem-solving skills, and prepare for assessments, while also supporting teachers with insights into student progress. The platform is integrated into the Learn on.ai initiative as one of the core learning tools used in participating schools. It is implemented directly in classroom and independent learning settings to demonstrate how AI can enhance mathematics education at scale. Learn on.ai is a national initiative by the Devbridge Foundation aimed at introducing AI-powered educational tools into Lithuanian schools. The project supports teachers and students by providing access to innovative digital solutions, fostering personalised learning, and reducing regional disparities in access to modern educational technologies.

column2: Personalised Mathematics Learning Platform for Every Student (Grades 5–12)
column4: This platform delivers personalised mathematics learning through adaptive tasks, interactive content, and gamified elements that respond to each student’s individual progress and knowledge gaps. It is designed to improve engagement, reinforce foundational skills, and support steady academic development. The platform is integrated withitn the Learn on.ai initiative, and it is used to demonstrate how artificial intelligence can individualise learning at scale. It is implemented in participating schools as a key tool for supporting diverse learning needs and helping students progress at their own pace. Learn on.ai is a Devbridge Foundation initiative that brings AI-driven educational solutions into real classroom environments. By equipping schools with innovative tools and teacher training, the project aims to modernize education, enhance learning outcomes, and ensure equal access to high-quality digital learning resources.

column2: EditAI – AI Tool for Teachers
column4: EditAI is an AI-powered tool designed to help teachers create lesson plans, assignments, and learning materials more efficiently. It supports modern teaching approaches such as problem-based and personalised learning, while allowing educators to adapt content to different student needs. EditAI is integrated into the Learn on.ai initiative as a teacher-focused solution that supports daily classroom work. It enables educators in participating schools to experiment with AI-assisted content creation and reduce administrative workload while improving lesson quality. The Learn on.ai initiative, led by the Devbridge Foundation, aims to introduce practical AI tools into education by working directly with schools and teachers. Through this program, educators gain access to innovative technologies that enhance teaching effectiveness, promote student engagement, and support long-term digital transformation in education.

column2: AI solution in Roskilde Municipality helps create minutes of consultation responses
column4: Roskilde Municipality uses generative AI to automatically create summaries of consultation responses from residents regarding planning decisions. When citizens submit feedback on municipal projects, employees manually had to summarize each response—a time-consuming and variable process. The AI solution uses Azure GPT to generate initial drafts of these summaries, which employees then review and refine to ensure accuracy. Testing shows the AI produces summaries of 75-80% quality initially, potentially saving up to one hour per consultation response. This allows staff to redirect time toward other citizen-facing tasks.

column2: AI helps the Danish Digital Agency conduct analyses
column4: Since 2021, large Danish companies must address data ethics in their annual reports. The Digital Agency needed to analyze thousands of reports to strengthen national data ethics guidance. The sheer volume—3,673 reports totaling approximately 88,000 pages from 2022-2023—made manual analysis impractical. The agency developed a generative AI solution using ChatGPT API with a custom prompt containing 30 classification categories. This enables systematic analysis of data ethics policies across entire company populations rather than just samples, allowing faster, more comprehensive insights for policy development.

column2: AI solution in ATP writes journal notes of conversations with citizens
column4: ATP, Denmark's labor market fund, receives approximately 2.5 million citizen calls annually, each requiring documentation as journal notes. The automated journaling solution converts call recordings to text, then summarizes conversations using generative AI. Results show the system generates draft notes in 5 seconds, with employees spending under 2 minutes correcting them in 70% of cases. This automation frees employees from time-consuming documentation tasks while maintaining consistent, high-quality records of citizen interactions.
